Metadata.title: LumenLingo — Master Languages Through Immersive Experiences
Metadata.description: Download LumenLingo, the premium language learning app for iPhone. Beautiful flashcards, adaptive practice, ambient soundscapes, and more. Free on the App Store.

Hero.headline: Master Languages <gradient>Through Immersive</gradient> Experiences
Hero.subheadline: A visual journey through language learning. Beautiful flashcards, ambient soundscapes, and breathing exercises — designed to make every session feel like a meditation.

FeatureShowcase.heading: Everything You Need to Master a New Language
FeatureShowcase.description: Six powerful pillars designed to transform how you learn, practice, and retain new languages.
FeatureShowcase.immersiveFlashcardsDesc: Beautifully designed cards with spaced repetition, swipe gestures, and visual feedback that makes memorisation feel effortless.
FeatureShowcase.smartPracticeDesc: Three distinct modes — multiple choice, word builder, and grammar quizzes — that adapt to your level and keep you engaged.
FeatureShowcase.richSoundscapesDesc: 12 ambient environments — from tropical rain to deep space — that reduce anxiety and boost focus during study sessions.
FeatureShowcase.breathingOrbsDesc: Guided breathing exercises with mesmerising visual orbs that calm the mind before or between practice sessions.
FeatureShowcase.languagePairsDesc: Learn in any direction across 9 languages. Study Spanish from English, Japanese from German, or any combination.
FeatureShowcase.progressTrackingDesc: Detailed analytics, daily streaks, XP milestones, and mastery indicators that show exactly how far you've come.

ScreenshotGallery.heading: Designed to Inspire
ScreenshotGallery.description: Every screen crafted with intention. Swipe through and see the attention to detail that makes LumenLingo unique.

Differentiators.heading: Why LumenLingo?
Differentiators.description: More than a language app — it's a mindful learning experience designed to be as beautiful as it is effective.
Differentiators.designDesc: Every pixel crafted with care. Dark-mode-first, silk-smooth animations, and a cosmic aesthetic that makes learning feel premium.
Differentiators.environmentsDesc: 12 unique soundscapes and animated backgrounds transform your study session into a meditative retreat.
Differentiators.practiceModesDesc: Flashcards, grammar quizzes, and word builder — each with unique mechanics, visual feedback, and adaptive difficulty.
Differentiators.mindfulnessDesc: Breathing orbs, ambient sounds, and calming visuals reduce anxiety and create the perfect headspace for learning.

values.beautyDesc: We believe tools for the mind should be beautiful. Every pixel, every animation, every interaction is designed to delight.
values.scienceDesc: Spaced repetition, active recall, and interleaving — grounded in decades of cognitive science research.
values.privacyDesc: Your data stays on your device and in your iCloud. We don't sell data, track behaviour, or serve ads.

timeline.sparkDesc: First working prototype built — a weekend hackathon that refused to stay a weekend. The mission: make language learning feel beautiful.

timeline.growingDesc: New practice modes, community features, and the App Store launch. Built by a solo developer with the ambition of a studio and the attention to detail of an artisan.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: Read how Lumenshore handles your data with care across the LumenLingo iOS app and lumenlingo.com website. Privacy-first approach, on-device processing, GDPR, CCPA and your rights.

hero.heading: Privacy Policy
hero.subtitle: Your privacy matters deeply to us. Here’s exactly how we handle your data across the LumenLingo app and website — in plain English, not legal jargon.

overview.heading: Overview
overview.p1: Lumenshore Limited (“Lumenshore”, “we”, “us”, or “our”), company number 09607326, registered at Windsor House, Troon Way Business Centre, Humberstone Lane, Leicester, England, LE4 9HA, operates the LumenLingo mobile application (the “App”) and the lumenlingo.com website (the “Website”). This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect information when you use either platform.
overview.p2: We built LumenLingo with a privacy-first architecture. On the iOS app, your learning data is processed and stored on your device and in your personal iCloud account — not on our servers. On the website, we collect minimal data necessary to operate the service and improve your experience. We believe your data is yours, and it should stay that way.
overview.p3: This policy is organised into two sections — Website and iOS App — so you can quickly find the information relevant to you.

websiteData.heading: Website Data Collection
websiteData.intro: When you visit lumenlingo.com, we may collect the following data:
websiteData.li1: Email address — if you sign up for our newsletter or join the waitlist. We collect your email solely to send you product updates and launch notifications. You can unsubscribe at any time.
websiteData.li2: Language preference — if you join the waitlist, we record which language you’re interested in to tailor future communications.
websiteData.li3: Campaign attribution (UTM parameters) — we capture UTM parameters (source, medium, campaign) from URLs in your browser’s sessionStorage to understand which marketing channels are effective. This data is not tied to your identity and is cleared when you close your browser tab.
websiteData.li4: Anonymous page views — we use Vercel Analytics, a cookie-free, privacy-respecting analytics service, to count page visits. No personal data is collected, no cookies are set, and no individual users are identified.
websiteData.li5: Anonymous performance metrics — we use Vercel Speed Insights to measure page load times and core web vitals. This data is fully anonymous and helps us keep the website fast.
websiteData.li6: Error data and session replays — we use Sentry for error monitoring. With your consent, Sentry may record session replays to help us diagnose and fix bugs. See the ‘Sentry Session Replay’ section below for full details.
websiteData.li7: IP addresses — your IP address is processed transiently by our API routes for rate limiting (to prevent abuse). IP addresses are not stored or logged by us.

sentryReplay.heading: Sentry Session Replay
sentryReplay.p1: We use Sentry (operated by Functional Software, Inc.) for error monitoring on our website. Sentry’s session replay feature records page interactions (clicks, scrolls, navigation) to help us reproduce and fix bugs.
sentryReplay.p2: Sampling rates: Session replays are captured for approximately 0.1% of normal browsing sessions. When an error occurs, the replay capture rate increases to 100% for that session only — this helps us understand exactly what led to the bug.
sentryReplay.p3: What is captured: Page navigation, clicks, scrolls, and screen content. All text inputs in forms (such as newsletter and waitlist fields) are masked to protect your privacy. Media content is blocked from capture.
sentryReplay.p4: What is sent: Replay data is transmitted to Sentry’s servers in the United States. It is retained according to Sentry’s data retention policy (typically 90 days).
sentryReplay.p5: Your control: Session replays are only active if you have given consent via our cookie consent banner. You can change your preference at any time.

vercelAnalytics.heading: Vercel Analytics & Speed Insights
vercelAnalytics.p1: We use Vercel Analytics and Vercel Speed Insights on our website. These services are privacy-focused by design:
vercelAnalytics.li1: No cookies — Vercel Analytics does not set any cookies on your device.
vercelAnalytics.li2: No personal data — no IP addresses, device fingerprints, or user identifiers are collected.
vercelAnalytics.li3: Aggregate only — we see total page view counts and performance metrics, but we cannot identify individual visitors.
vercelAnalytics.p2: Both services are operated by Vercel Inc. Data is processed on Vercel’s infrastructure. See <vercelPrivacy>Vercel’s Privacy Policy</vercelPrivacy> for more details.

customEvents.heading: Custom Analytics Events
customEvents.intro: In addition to page views, we track the following anonymised events through Vercel Analytics to understand how visitors use our website. No personal data is attached to these events:
customEvents.li1: app_store_click — when a visitor clicks an App Store download link (records link location on the page)
customEvents.li2: pricing_view — when a visitor views the pricing page
customEvents.li3: pricing_cta_click — when a visitor clicks a pricing call-to-action button (records which tier)
customEvents.li4: blog_post_read — when a visitor reads most of a blog post (records the post slug and title)
customEvents.li5: feature_section_view — when a visitor views a feature section on the homepage
customEvents.li6: newsletter_signup — when a visitor signs up for the newsletter (records the page location, not the email)
customEvents.li7: demo_started — when a visitor starts the interactive flashcard demo
customEvents.li8: demo_completed — when a visitor completes the demo (records correct/total scores)
customEvents.li9: demo_cta_click — when a visitor clicks a call-to-action after completing the demo
customEvents.li10: page_not_found — when a visitor lands on a 404 page (records the path)
customEvents.li11: error_page_view — when a visitor sees an error page (records the error message)

serviceWorker.heading: Offline Caching (Service Worker)
serviceWorker.p1: Our website uses a Service Worker to cache static assets (HTML pages, CSS stylesheets, JavaScript bundles, images, and fonts) on your device. This enables faster page loads on repeat visits and limited offline access.
serviceWorker.p2: The Service Worker cache is stored entirely on your device and contains no personal data. You can clear it at any time through your browser’s settings (Clear site data). No cached data is sent back to our servers.

appData.heading: iOS App Data Collection
appData.intro: The LumenLingo iOS app collects minimal data necessary to provide a great learning experience:
appData.li1: Learning progress data — flashcard mastery levels, practice scores, streaks, XP, and session history. This data is stored locally on your device and synced via iCloud.
appData.li2: App preferences — your selected language pairs, soundscape preferences, visual background choices, and tier selection. Stored on-device and synced via iCloud.
appData.li3: Subscription status — your membership tier, managed entirely by Apple through the App Store. We receive a subscription status from Apple but do not process payment information.
appData.li4: Anonymous analytics — we may collect aggregated, non-identifiable usage metrics (such as which features are most popular) to improve the app. No personal data is included.

whatWeDontCollect.heading: What We Don’t Collect
whatWeDontCollect.intro: Across both our app and website, we want to be clear about what we never collect or do:
whatWeDontCollect.li1: No location data or GPS coordinates
whatWeDontCollect.li2: No contacts, photos, or access to other apps on your device
whatWeDontCollect.li3: No payment or credit card information (Apple handles all app billing)
whatWeDontCollect.li4: No advertising identifiers or tracking pixels
whatWeDontCollect.li5: No data sold or shared with third-party advertisers — ever
whatWeDontCollect.li6: No personal data shared with AI model training services

legalBasis.heading: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.intro: Under GDPR and UK GDPR, we process personal data only when we have a lawful basis. Here is the legal basis for each type of data we handle:
legalBasis.consentLi1: Newsletter signup — you actively provide your email and opt in to receive updates
legalBasis.consentLi2: Waitlist signup — you actively provide your email and language preference
legalBasis.consentLi3: Sentry session replay — replays are only captured after you give explicit consent via our cookie banner
legalBasis.legitimateLi1: Vercel Analytics — anonymous, cookie-free page view counting to improve our website. Our interest in understanding site usage is balanced against minimal privacy impact (no PII collected)
legalBasis.legitimateLi2: Sentry error monitoring — collecting error diagnostics to maintain service reliability. No user behaviour is captured without consent; only crash and error data is collected under this basis
legalBasis.legitimateLi3: Rate limiting (IP address processing) — preventing abuse of our API endpoints. IP addresses are processed transiently and not stored
legalBasis.contractLi1: iOS app learning data — processing your learning progress is necessary to deliver the language-learning service you use
legalBasis.contractLi2: iCloud sync — syncing your progress across devices is a core part of the service
legalBasis.contractLi3: Subscription management — verifying your tier to provide the appropriate features

dataProvisionRequirements.heading: Whether Providing Data Is Obligatory
dataProvisionRequirements.p1: Under UK GDPR Article 13(2)(e), we must inform you whether the provision of personal data is a statutory or contractual requirement, and the possible consequences of failing to provide such data:
dataProvisionRequirements.contractualLi1: Account creation data (email address via Clerk authentication) — this is a contractual requirement. Without it, we cannot create your account or provide the Service.
dataProvisionRequirements.contractualLi2: Subscription payment data (processed by Apple/RevenueCat) — this is a contractual requirement for premium features. Without it, we cannot process your payment or grant premium access.
dataProvisionRequirements.voluntaryLi1: Analytics consent (Vercel Analytics, Sentry Session Replay) — this is entirely voluntary. Declining has no impact on your ability to use the Service.
dataProvisionRequirements.voluntaryLi2: Feedback and support emails — voluntary. If you do not provide contact details, we may be unable to respond to your query.
dataProvisionRequirements.statutoryP1: There is no statutory (legal) requirement to provide your personal data to us. All data provision is either contractual (necessary for the service) or voluntary.

dataFlow.heading: Where Your Data Goes
dataFlow.intro: Here is a plain-English summary of where your data is stored and processed:
dataFlow.li1: Your device — all iOS app learning data, preferences, and progress are stored locally on your iPhone or iPad using Apple’s SwiftData framework
dataFlow.li2: Your iCloud account — learning data syncs across your Apple devices via your personal iCloud account, encrypted by Apple. We have no access to this data
dataFlow.li3: Vercel (website hosting) — our website is hosted on Vercel’s global edge network. Anonymous analytics data is processed by Vercel. Vercel’s servers are primarily located in the United States with edge nodes worldwide
dataFlow.li4: Sentry (error monitoring) — error logs and (with your consent) session replay data are sent to Sentry’s servers in the United States. Sentry processes this data under their DPA with Standard Contractual Clauses
dataFlow.li5: Apple (App Store & iCloud) — subscription management and iCloud sync are handled by Apple’s global infrastructure
dataFlow.noOtherParties: Your data is not sent to any other third parties. We do not use Google Analytics, Facebook SDK, or any advertising networks.

howWeUse.heading: How We Use Your Information
howWeUse.intro: The limited data we handle is used solely to:
howWeUse.li1: Personalise your learning — spaced repetition scheduling, difficulty adaptation, and progress tracking depend on your learning history.
howWeUse.li2: Sync your progress — iCloud sync ensures your data follows you across all your Apple devices.
howWeUse.li3: Manage your subscription — we verify your membership tier to unlock the appropriate features.
howWeUse.li4: Improve the app and website — anonymous aggregate data helps us understand which features are valuable and where to focus development.
howWeUse.li5: Communicate with you — if you signed up for our newsletter or waitlist, we use your email to send product updates. You can unsubscribe at any time.
howWeUse.li6: Diagnose and fix bugs — Sentry error data (and session replays with your consent) help us identify and resolve issues quickly.

dataStorage.heading: Data Storage & Security
dataStorage.intro: LumenLingo uses a privacy-first, on-device architecture:
dataStorage.li1: On-device storage — all learning data is stored locally using Apple’s SwiftData framework, protected by your device’s built-in encryption.
dataStorage.li2: iCloud sync — data syncs between your devices via your personal iCloud account, encrypted end-to-end by Apple. We do not have access to your iCloud data.
dataStorage.li3: No external servers — LumenLingo does not maintain servers that store your personal data from the iOS app. There is no cloud database for us to secure (or breach).
dataStorage.li4: App Store security — subscription management is handled by Apple’s secure infrastructure.
dataStorage.li5: Website security — lumenlingo.com uses HTTPS encryption, strict Content Security Policy headers, and rate-limited API endpoints to protect your data.

thirdParty.heading: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.intro: LumenLingo integrates with a limited number of third-party services:
thirdParty.li1: Apple App Store & iCloud — for app distribution, subscription management, payment processing, and data synchronisation. Subject to Apple’s Privacy Policy.
thirdParty.li2: Vercel — for website hosting, anonymous analytics, and performance monitoring. Cookie-free, GDPR-compliant. Subject to Vercel’s Privacy Policy.
thirdParty.li3: Sentry — for website error monitoring and (with consent) session replay. Subject to Sentry’s Privacy Policy.
thirdParty.noAdNetworks: We do not use Google Analytics, Facebook SDK, or any advertising networks.
thirdParty.li4: Clerk — for user authentication and identity management. Subject to Clerk's Privacy Policy.
thirdParty.li5: RevenueCat — for subscription management and entitlement tracking. Apple remains the merchant of record for all payments. Subject to RevenueCat's Privacy Policy.

childrensPrivacy.heading: Children’s Privacy
childrensPrivacy.p1: LumenLingo is designed for users aged 13 and older. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 (or the applicable minimum age in your jurisdiction, which may be higher — for example, 16 in Germany and the Netherlands).
childrensPrivacy.p2: The iOS app does not require account registration and stores data locally, so no personal information is transmitted to us from app users of any age. On the website, we do not knowingly accept newsletter or waitlist signups from anyone under 13.
childrensPrivacy.p3: Parents and guardians can manage or delete the app and its data at any time through standard iOS device management. If you believe a child has provided personal data to us, please contact us at legal@lumenlingo.com and we will delete it promptly.
childrensPrivacy.coppaP1: LumenLingo complies with the Children’s Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA). We do not knowingly collect, use, or disclose personal information from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent. Our service is not directed at children under 13, and we do not target advertising to children.
childrensPrivacy.coppaP2: All website forms that collect personal information (such as email addresses) include an age-confirmation step requiring users to confirm they are at least 13 years old, or that they have parental or guardian consent if under 18.
childrensPrivacy.parentalP1: Under COPPA and similar laws, parents and guardians have the right to:
childrensPrivacy.parentalLi1: Review any personal information we may have inadvertently collected from their child
childrensPrivacy.parentalLi2: Request deletion of their child’s personal information from our systems
childrensPrivacy.parentalLi3: Refuse further collection or use of their child’s information
childrensPrivacy.parentalLi4: Manage or remove the app and its locally stored data through standard iOS device management
childrensPrivacy.parentalP2: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>. If we discover that we have collected personal information from a child under 13 without proper parental consent, we will delete that information promptly.
childrensPrivacy.ferpaP1: LumenLingo is not administered by or on behalf of educational institutions. If a school or educational organisation chooses to recommend LumenLingo to students, it does so independently. The institution is responsible for ensuring compliance with the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) and for obtaining any required parental consent before directing students under 13 to use the service.

dataRetention.heading: Data Retention & Deletion
dataRetention.intro: We only keep your data for as long as necessary:
dataRetention.websiteLi1: Newsletter/waitlist emails — retained until you unsubscribe, plus up to 30 days to process your removal.
dataRetention.websiteLi2: Sentry error logs — retained by Sentry for 90 days, then automatically deleted.
dataRetention.websiteLi3: Sentry session replays — retained by Sentry for 90 days, then automatically deleted.
dataRetention.websiteLi4: Vercel Analytics — aggregated, cookie-free analytics data is retained for 30 days from collection, then automatically purged. No personally identifiable information is stored.
dataRetention.appLi1: Delete account — use the in-app Settings → Sign Out → Delete Account to permanently erase all learning data, progress, preferences, and iCloud sync data. This action cannot be undone.
dataRetention.appLi2: Delete the app — uninstalling LumenLingo removes all locally stored data.
dataRetention.appLi3: iCloud data — you can manage iCloud storage through your device’s Settings → Apple ID → iCloud → Manage Storage.
dataRetention.appLi4: Subscription — cancel your subscription through Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions. No data is retained by us after cancellation.
dataRetention.websiteLi5: Consent records — retained for 3 years after your last interaction, as required for GDPR accountability.
dataRetention.websiteLi6: Data request logs — retained for 3 years after completion, as required for GDPR accountability.

ccpaNotice.heading: California Notice at Collection (CCPA/CPRA)
ccpaNotice.intro: If you are a California resident, the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA) require that we disclose the categories of personal information we collect, the purposes for collection, and whether such information is sold or shared. This notice is provided at or before the point of collection.

identifiers.dataCollected: Email address (newsletter and waitlist signups on the website)
identifiers.purpose: To send product updates, language learning tips, and waitlist notifications
identifiers.retention: Until you unsubscribe, plus up to 30 days

internetActivity.category: Internet or Other Electronic Network Activity
internetActivity.dataCollected: Page views, click events, error logs, session replays (with consent)
internetActivity.purpose: Website analytics, bug detection, performance monitoring, and improving the user experience

education.dataCollected: Language learning progress, practice scores, mastered vocabulary (iOS app only, stored on-device)
education.purpose: To personalise your learning experience and track progress
education.retention: Until you delete the app or your account

audioVisual.category: Audio, Electronic, Visual, or Similar Information

doNotSell.heading: Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information
doNotSell.p1: Lumenshore does not sell your personal information to third parties. We do not share your personal information for cross-context behavioural advertising. Because we do not engage in these practices, there is no need to opt out — but we provide this disclosure as required by the CCPA/CPRA.
doNotSell.p2: If our practices ever change, we will update this policy and provide a mechanism for you to opt out before any sale or sharing begins.
doNotSell.p3: California residents may also use the <gpcLink>Global Privacy Control (GPC)</gpcLink> browser signal to communicate their opt-out preference. We honour GPC as a valid opt-out request under the CPRA.

breachNotification.heading: Data Breach Notification
breachNotification.p1: In the event of a personal data breach that is likely to result in a risk to your rights and freedoms, we will notify the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) without undue delay and, where feasible, within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach, in accordance with UK GDPR Article 33. Where notification to the ICO is not made within 72 hours, it shall be accompanied by reasons for the delay.
breachNotification.p2: If the breach is likely to result in a high risk to your rights and freedoms, we will also notify you directly without undue delay, in accordance with UK GDPR Article 34. We will communicate to you in clear and plain language:
breachNotification.li1: The nature of the personal data breach, including where possible the categories and approximate number of data subjects and records concerned
breachNotification.li2: The name and contact details of our data protection lead from whom you can obtain more information
breachNotification.li3: The likely consequences of the breach
breachNotification.li4: The measures taken or proposed to address the breach, including, where appropriate, measures to mitigate its possible adverse effects
breachNotification.p3: We maintain internal breach response procedures, including a breach register, to ensure we can detect, report, and investigate personal data breaches effectively. For questions about our breach notification procedures, contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>.

automatedDecisions.heading: Automated Decision-Making and Profiling
automatedDecisions.p1: We do not use your personal data for automated decision-making that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects on you, as defined by UK GDPR Article 22.
automatedDecisions.p2: Our app uses a spaced-repetition algorithm to optimise your learning schedule. This is pedagogical optimisation only — it determines when to show you a flashcard, not any decision with legal or similarly significant effects on you.
automatedDecisions.p3: If we ever introduce automated decision-making with legal or significant effects in the future, we will update this policy and ensure you have the right to: (a) obtain human intervention, (b) express your point of view, and (c) contest the decision, as required by UK GDPR Article 22(3).

yourRights.heading: Your Rights
yourRights.intro: Depending on your location, you may have additional rights regarding your data:
yourRights.gdprLi1: Right to access your data — app data is on your device and iCloud; for website data, contact us.
yourRights.gdprLi2: Right to rectification — edit app preferences directly; for website data, contact us.
yourRights.gdprLi3: Right to erasure — use the in-app Delete Account feature, delete the app, or contact us to remove your email from our lists.
yourRights.gdprLi4: Right to data portability — export all your personal data as a free JSON download from Settings → Export My Data in the iOS app. Your data is always stored in standard formats. Contact us for website data export.
yourRights.gdprLi5: Right to object — contact us to opt out of anonymous analytics or to object to processing based on legitimate interest.
yourRights.gdprLi6: Right to restrict processing — contact us to request that we limit how we process your data.
yourRights.gdprLi7: Right to lodge a complaint — you can complain to the <icoLink>Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO)</icoLink> or your local supervisory authority.
yourRights.gdprLi8: Right to withdraw consent — where we rely on consent as a legal basis (e.g., analytics), you may withdraw your consent at any time by adjusting your cookie preferences or contacting us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out before the withdrawal.
yourRights.ccpaLi1: Right to know — you may request that we disclose the categories and specific pieces of personal information we have collected about you. See the Notice at Collection section above.
yourRights.ccpaLi2: Right to delete — you may request deletion of your personal information. Use the in-app Delete Account feature, delete the app, or contact us to remove website data.
yourRights.ccpaLi3: Right to correct — you may request correction of inaccurate personal information. App data can be edited directly; for website data, contact us.
yourRights.ccpaLi4: Right to opt out of sale or sharing — we do not sell or share personal information for cross-context behavioural advertising. No opt-out action is required.
yourRights.ccpaLi5: Right to limit use of sensitive personal information — we do not collect sensitive personal information as defined by the CCPA/CPRA.
yourRights.ccpaLi6: Non-discrimination — we will not deny you services, charge different prices, or provide a different quality of service because you exercised your privacy rights.
yourRights.ccpaResponse: For California residents, we will acknowledge your request within 10 business days and respond substantively within 45 calendar days. If more time is needed, we may extend by an additional 45 days with notice. We may verify your identity by matching information you provide with data we have on file.
yourRights.usStateHeading: Under US State Privacy Laws (Virginia, Colorado, Connecticut & Others)
yourRights.usStateIntro: If you reside in Virginia, Colorado, Connecticut, Utah, or other US states with comprehensive privacy laws, you hold similar rights to those described under CCPA/CPRA above. These laws include the Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act (VCDPA), Colorado Privacy Act (CPA), and Connecticut Data Privacy Act (CTDPA).
yourRights.usStateLi1: Right to access — you may confirm whether we process your personal data and request a copy.
yourRights.usStateLi2: Right to correct — you may request correction of inaccurate personal data.
yourRights.usStateLi3: Right to delete — you may request deletion of your personal data.
yourRights.usStateLi4: Right to data portability — you may obtain your data in a portable, readily usable format.
yourRights.usStateLi5: Right to opt out — you may opt out of targeted advertising, the sale of personal data, and profiling. Lumenshore does not engage in any of these practices.
yourRights.usStateGpc: Colorado and Connecticut require us to honour universal opt-out signals such as <gpcLink>Global Privacy Control (GPC)</gpcLink>. We honour GPC across all jurisdictions.
yourRights.usStateAppeal: If we decline your request, you may appeal by contacting us at legal@lumenlingo.com. We will respond to appeals within 60 days. If you are unsatisfied with the outcome, you may contact your state attorney general's office.
yourRights.verificationP1: To protect your data, we will verify your identity before processing a rights request. We may ask you to confirm the email address associated with your account or subscription, or to provide other identifying details that match our records.
yourRights.verificationP2: You may designate an authorised agent to make a request on your behalf. We will require proof of the agent's authority (such as a signed authorisation or power of attorney) and may still verify your identity directly.
yourRights.timeframesCcpa: CCPA/CPRA (California) — acknowledgement within 10 business days; substantive response within 45 calendar days (extendable by an additional 45 days with notice).
yourRights.timeframesVcdpa: VCDPA (Virginia) — response within 45 days (extendable by an additional 45 days with notice).
yourRights.timeframesCpa: CPA (Colorado) — response within 45 days (extendable by an additional 45 days when reasonably necessary).
yourRights.timeframesCtdpa: CTDPA (Connecticut) — response within 45 days (extendable by an additional 45 days with notice).
yourRights.timeframesGdpr: GDPR (EEA & UK) — response within one calendar month (extendable by two further months for complex requests).
yourRights.californiaP1: Under California Civil Code § 1798.83 ("Shine the Light"), California residents may request information about the disclosure of personal information to third parties for direct marketing purposes. Lumenshore does not disclose personal information to third parties for their direct marketing purposes.
yourRights.californiaP2: To exercise any California privacy right — including rights under the CCPA/CPRA — email us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink> with the subject line "California Privacy Rights" or submit a request through our <dataRequestLink>Data Request</dataRequestLink> page.
yourRights.exerciseRights: To exercise any of these rights, email us at legal@lumenlingo.com or submit a request through our <dataRequestLink>Data Request</dataRequestLink> page. We will respond within the applicable timeframe for your jurisdiction. We may need to verify your identity before processing certain requests.

policyUpdates.heading: Policy Updates
policyUpdates.p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time, typically to reflect changes in our services or legal requirements. When we make significant changes, we’ll update the “Last updated” date and version number at the top of this page.
policyUpdates.p2: We encourage you to review this page periodically. Continued use of LumenLingo after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
policyUpdates.v2: v2.0 (23 March 2026) — Extended policy to cover lumenlingo.com website data collection, added Sentry session replay disclosure, Vercel Analytics disclosure, legal basis for processing, data flow section, and service worker caching details.
policyUpdates.v1: v1.0 (22 March 2026) — Initial privacy policy covering the LumenLingo iOS app.
policyUpdates.v2_1: v2.1 (26 March 2026) — Reorganised sections: moved commercial/regulatory compliance disclosures (VAT treatment, EU VAT, international tax, pricing compliance, sanctions, encryption export controls) to Terms of Service. Added cross-reference to Terms. Consolidated trademark attribution to shared Legal namespace. No content was removed.
policyUpdates.v2_2: v2.2 (July 2025) — Phase 2 legal hardening: standardised company name to Lumenshore Limited throughout; added GDPR Art. 77 ICO complaint right and contact details; added processor sub-processor register; enhanced international transfer safeguards; added regional privacy frameworks (Japan APPI, China PIPL, Brazil LGPD, Germany BDSG, Australia Privacy Act, India DPDP, Canada PIPEDA, South Korea PIPA, Switzerland FADP); improved electronic communications and PECR compliance disclosures.

contactUs.heading: Contact Us
contactUs.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, your data, or want to exercise your rights, please reach out:
contactUs.company: Company: Lumenshore Limited, Windsor House, Troon Way Business Centre, Humberstone Lane, Leicester, England, LE4 9HA
contactUs.responseTime: We typically respond within 48 hours and within 30 days for formal data rights requests.

subProcessors.heading: Sub-Processors
subProcessors.intro: The following third-party services process data on our behalf. Each sub-processor has been assessed for data protection compliance:

apple.purpose: App distribution, subscription billing, data sync

vercel.purpose: Website hosting, anonymous analytics, speed insights

sentry.dataProcessed: Error logs, stack traces, session replays (with consent)

clerk.dataProcessed: Email address, user identifier, authentication tokens

revenueCat.dataProcessed: App user ID, subscription status, purchase receipts (via Apple — RevenueCat does not receive raw payment card data)

dpaStatus.heading: Data Processing Agreements
dpaStatus.p1: We maintain Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) or equivalent contractual protections with all sub-processors that handle personal data:
dpaStatus.li1: Apple — covered by Apple's standard DPA for developers, incorporating Standard Contractual Clauses for international transfers.
dpaStatus.li2: Vercel — covered by Vercel's DPA, available at vercel.com/legal/dpa. Vercel Analytics is cookie-free and processes no personal data.
dpaStatus.li3: Sentry — covered by Sentry's DPA with Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) for EU-US data transfers.
dpaStatus.li4: Clerk — covered by Clerk's Data Processing Agreement, incorporating Standard Contractual Clauses and EU-US Data Privacy Framework certification for international transfers.
dpaStatus.li5: RevenueCat — covered by RevenueCat's Data Processing Addendum with Standard Contractual Clauses. RevenueCat does not receive raw payment card data; Apple is the merchant of record.

changes.heading: Sub-Processor Changes
changes.p1: If we add or change a sub-processor that handles personal data, we will update this page and note the change in the Version History section of this Privacy Policy. For material changes, we will provide at least 30 days' notice before the new sub-processor begins processing data.

internationalTransfers.heading: International Data Transfers
internationalTransfers.intro: Lumenshore Limited is based in the United Kingdom. Some of your data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside the UK and the European Economic Area (EEA). Here is where each service processes data and the legal mechanism that protects the transfer:
internationalTransfers.li1: Apple (United States & global) — iCloud data and App Store subscription data may be processed in Apple’s global data centres, including in the United States. Apple participates in transfers under Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
internationalTransfers.li2: Vercel (United States & global edge) — our website is served from Vercel’s edge network, with primary servers in the United States. Vercel Analytics processes no personal data. Website hosting data is protected under Vercel’s GDPR DPA with Standard Contractual Clauses.
internationalTransfers.li3: Sentry (United States) — error logs and session replay data (with your consent) are transmitted to Sentry’s servers in the United States. These transfers are protected by Sentry’s DPA incorporating Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs).

safeguards.heading: Transfer Safeguards
safeguards.intro: For all international data transfers, we ensure at least one of the following safeguards is in place:
safeguards.li1: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) — EU Commission-approved contractual clauses that bind the data importer to protect your data to GDPR standards.
safeguards.li2: EU-US Data Privacy Framework — where the recipient is certified under the framework, providing an adequacy basis for transfers to the US.
safeguards.li3: UK International Data Transfer Agreement (IDTA) — the UK equivalent of SCCs, approved by the ICO for transfers from the UK.
safeguards.li4: Adequacy decisions — where the European Commission or UK Government has determined that the destination country provides adequate data protection.

internationalTransfers.p1: You may request a copy of the relevant transfer safeguards by contacting us at legal@lumenlingo.com.
internationalTransfers.li4: Clerk (United States) — authentication data (email address, user identifiers, auth tokens) is processed on Clerk's servers in the United States. These transfers are protected by Clerk's DPA incorporating Standard Contractual Clauses and Clerk's EU-US Data Privacy Framework certification.
internationalTransfers.li5: RevenueCat (United States) — subscription management data (app user ID, subscription status, Apple purchase receipts) is processed on RevenueCat's servers in the United States. These transfers are protected by RevenueCat's Data Processing Addendum with Standard Contractual Clauses.

aggregateData.heading: Aggregated and De-identified Data
aggregateData.p1: We may collect, aggregate, and de-identify personal data so that it can no longer reasonably identify any individual. Once de-identified, such data is no longer personal data under GDPR, UK GDPR, CCPA, or any applicable data protection law.
aggregateData.p2: We may use, disclose, and publish de-identified and aggregated data for any purpose, including but not limited to:

deIdentification.heading: De-identification Standards
deIdentification.p1: Our de-identification process meets the following legal standards:
deIdentification.li1: GDPR Recital 26 — data cannot be singled out, linked, or inferred to identify any individual, taking into account all means reasonably likely to be used.
deIdentification.li2: CCPA § 1798.140(m) — information that cannot reasonably be used to infer information about, or otherwise be linked to, a particular consumer or household. We maintain a commitment not to attempt to re-identify de-identified data.

examples.heading: Examples of Aggregate Data
examples.p1: Examples of the types of aggregate data we may derive and publish include:
examples.li1: Total number of users per language pair
examples.li5: Feature usage statistics across membership tiers
examples.p2: No individual user can be identified from any published aggregate data.

aggregateData.survival: Our right to retain and use Aggregate Data survives account deletion and service termination. After you delete your account, we may retain Aggregate Data that was derived from your use of the Service.

gpcSignals.heading: Global Privacy Control & Do Not Track
gpcSignals.p1: We honour the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal. When your browser sends a GPC signal (via the Sec-GPC HTTP header or the navigator.globalPrivacyControl JavaScript API), we treat it as a valid opt-out request for non-essential data processing.
gpcSignals.p2: Under the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), GPC constitutes a valid opt-out of the sale or sharing of personal information. Under the Colorado Privacy Act and Connecticut Data Privacy Act, we are required to honour universal opt-out mechanisms including GPC.
gpcSignals.p3: When a GPC signal is detected, we automatically suppress all non-essential tracking on our website — including analytics events and session replay — regardless of any prior cookie consent preferences.
gpcSignals.p4: We also honour the Do Not Track (DNT) browser signal. Although DNT has been deprecated by the W3C, we treat it identically to GPC for consistency and as a demonstration of our commitment to privacy.

ukDataProtection.heading: UK Data Protection
ukDataProtection.intro: Lumenshore Limited is a company incorporated in England and Wales (Company No. 09607326). As a UK-based data controller, we comply with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) — retained from EU law via the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 — and the Data Protection Act 2018 (DPA 2018). These laws govern how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal data.
ukDataProtection.controllerP1: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:
ukDataProtection.controllerLi2: Registered Address: Windsor House, Troon Way Business Centre, Humberstone Lane, Leicester, England, LE4 9HA
ukDataProtection.dplP1: Although Lumenshore is not required to appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO) under Article 37 UK GDPR (we are not a public authority, do not carry out large-scale monitoring, and do not process special category data at scale), we have designated a data protection lead to oversee compliance:
ukDataProtection.dplP2: Data Protection Lead: The Director of Lumenshore Limited
ukDataProtection.dplP3: You can contact our data protection lead at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink> for any questions about how we handle your personal data, to exercise your data rights, or to raise a data protection concern.
ukDataProtection.icoP1: As required by section 25 of the Data Protection Act 2018, Lumenshore Limited pays the annual data protection fee to the <icoLink>Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO)</icoLink>, the UK’s independent supervisory authority for data protection.
ukDataProtection.icoP2: The ICO maintains a public register of fee payers. You can verify our registration status on the ICO’s website.
ukDataProtection.adequacyP1: When personal data is transferred between the UK and the European Economic Area (EEA), it is protected by the UK–EU adequacy decision made under Article 45 UK GDPR. The European Commission granted adequacy to the UK in June 2021, recognising that UK data protection law provides an essentially equivalent level of protection to the EU GDPR.
ukDataProtection.adequacyP2: For transfers to the United States, we rely on service providers certified under the EU–US Data Privacy Framework (DPF) and its UK Extension, which the UK government has recognised as providing adequate safeguards. Where a sub-processor is not DPF-certified, we ensure Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the ICO are in place.
ukDataProtection.adequacyP3: For further details on international transfers and the specific safeguards we use, see the <transfersLink>International Data Transfers</transfersLink> section of this policy.
ukDataProtection.specialCategoryP1: LumenLingo does not collect or process any special category data as defined by Article 9 UK GDPR and section 10 of the DPA 2018. This includes: racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data, health data, sex life or sexual orientation, and criminal conviction data.
ukDataProtection.specialCategoryP2: If our data processing activities change in the future to include any special category data, we will update this policy, conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA), and obtain explicit consent where required.
ukDataProtection.complaintHeading: Your Right to Complain to the ICO
ukDataProtection.complaintP1: If you are unhappy with how we have handled your personal data, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO). We would appreciate the opportunity to address your concerns first — please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink> and we will do our best to resolve the issue.
ukDataProtection.complaintP2: If you are not satisfied with our response, you can contact the ICO:
ukDataProtection.complaintLi3: Post: Information Commissioner’s Office, Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow, Cheshire, SK9 5AF
ukDataProtection.icoRegistration: Our ICO registration number is ZB718685. You can verify this on the <icoLink>ICO register</icoLink>.

electronicComms.heading: Electronic Communications (UK PECR)
electronicComms.intro: The Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations 2003 (PECR) set specific rules for electronic marketing and the use of cookies and similar technologies. As a UK company, Lumenshore Limited complies with PECR alongside the UK GDPR.
electronicComms.cookieP1: PECR Regulation 6 requires your consent before we store or access information on your device, except where it is strictly necessary to provide a service you have requested. Our approach:
electronicComms.cookieLi1: Strictly necessary storage — consent preferences and language settings — is set without consent because it is essential for the website to function as you have requested.
electronicComms.cookieLi2: Non-essential storage — analytics tracking, error monitoring, and session replay — is only activated after you give explicit consent via our cookie banner.
electronicComms.cookieLi3: You can change your preferences at any time using the “Cookie Settings” link in the website footer.
electronicComms.cookieLi4: If your browser sends a Global Privacy Control (GPC) or Do Not Track (DNT) signal, all non-essential storage is automatically suppressed.
electronicComms.cookieP2: For full details of what we store and why, see our <cookiePolicyLink>Cookie Policy</cookiePolicyLink>.
electronicComms.newsletterP1: We only send marketing emails to people who have given explicit, freely given consent by subscribing to our newsletter. We do not use pre-ticked boxes or bundle marketing consent with other agreements.
electronicComms.newsletterLi3: Describes the types of content you will receive (product updates, language learning tips, and occasional offers)
electronicComms.newsletterP3: We record your consent (including the version of the consent text shown, a timestamp, and a hashed identifier) for our accountability records.
electronicComms.softOptInP1: PECR Regulation 22(3) allows businesses to send marketing to existing customers for similar products without explicit consent (“soft opt-in”), provided an opt-out was offered at point of collection and in every subsequent message. Lumenshore does not currently rely on the soft opt-in exception — all marketing requires explicit consent.

japanPrivacy.heading: Japan — Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI)
japanPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo actively targets Japanese users through our Japanese (ja) locale and Japanese language pairs. Accordingly, we comply with Japan’s Act on the Protection of Personal Information (個人情報の保護に関する法律, APPI) as amended.
japanPrivacy.purposeP1: Under APPI, we clearly identify the purposes for which we use personal information. Lumenshore Limited uses your personal information for the following purposes:
japanPrivacy.purposeLi1: Service delivery — providing the LumenLingo language-learning service, including personalised learning content and progress tracking
japanPrivacy.purposeLi2: Service improvement — analysing anonymised usage patterns to improve app features and educational content
japanPrivacy.purposeLi3: Error monitoring — detecting and resolving technical issues via Sentry (with your consent on the website)
japanPrivacy.purposeLi4: Communications — responding to your enquiries and sending service-related notifications
japanPrivacy.purposeLi5: Legal compliance — fulfilling our obligations under applicable laws
japanPrivacy.handlerP1: The personal information handler responsible for your data is:
japanPrivacy.handlerLi2: Registered address: Windsor House, Troon Way Business Centre, Humberstone Lane, Leicester, England, LE4 9HA
japanPrivacy.crossBorderP1: Your personal information may be transferred to and processed in countries outside Japan. Under APPI, we inform you of the following cross-border transfers and the safeguards in place:
japanPrivacy.crossBorderLi1: United Kingdom — Lumenshore Limited is based in the UK, which maintains data protection standards recognised under the Japan–UK mutual adequacy arrangement
japanPrivacy.crossBorderLi2: United States (Vercel) — our website is hosted on Vercel’s edge network. We rely on Standard Contractual Clauses and the EU–US Data Privacy Framework as transfer safeguards
japanPrivacy.crossBorderLi3: United States (Sentry) — error monitoring data (with your consent) is processed by Sentry. We rely on Standard Contractual Clauses as transfer safeguards
japanPrivacy.crossBorderLi4: Apple (global) — iCloud data and App Store data are processed by Apple under Apple’s own data protection agreements and privacy commitments
japanPrivacy.crossBorderP2: Japan and the UK benefit from mutual adequacy recognition, meaning the transfer of personal data between these countries is permitted without additional safeguards.
japanPrivacy.rightsP1: As a data subject under APPI, you have the following rights regarding your retained personal data:
japanPrivacy.rightsLi1: Right to disclosure — request disclosure of your retained personal data held by us
japanPrivacy.rightsLi2: Right to correction — request correction, addition, or deletion of inaccurate personal data
japanPrivacy.rightsLi3: Right to cessation of use — request that we cease using or erase your personal data
japanPrivacy.rightsLi4: Right to cessation of third-party provision — request that we stop providing your personal data to third parties
japanPrivacy.rightsLi5: Right to receive usage records — request records of third-party provision of your personal data
japanPrivacy.rightsP2: We will respond to your requests without delay. To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>.
japanPrivacy.ppcP1: If you are unsatisfied with our handling of your personal information, you may lodge a complaint with Japan’s Personal Information Protection Commission (個人情報保護委員会):

chinaPrivacy.heading: China — Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL)
chinaPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo actively targets Chinese users through our Chinese (zh) locale and Chinese language pairs. Accordingly, we comply with China's Personal Information Protection Law (中华人民共和国个人信息保护法, PIPL) which took effect on 1 November 2021.
chinaPrivacy.applicabilityP1: PIPL applies to the processing of personal information of individuals within the People's Republic of China, including by overseas entities that provide products or services to individuals in China, or analyse and assess the behaviour of individuals in China. LumenLingo's Chinese locale, Chinese language pairs, and localised content constitute deliberate targeting of individuals in China.
chinaPrivacy.legalBasesP1: Under PIPL, we process your personal information on the following legal bases:
chinaPrivacy.legalBasesLi1: Consent — we obtain your informed, voluntary consent before processing personal information. Where required, we obtain separate consent for sensitive data processing, cross-border transfers, and provision of data to third parties
chinaPrivacy.legalBasesLi2: Contract performance — processing necessary to enter into or perform a contract to which you are a party (e.g., providing the LumenLingo service)
chinaPrivacy.legalBasesLi3: Legal obligations — processing necessary to fulfil our statutory duties and obligations
chinaPrivacy.legalBasesLi4: Legitimate interests — processing within a reasonable scope for our legitimate interests, provided it does not substantially affect your rights and interests
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderP1: Your personal information may be transferred outside the People's Republic of China. Under PIPL, we implement the following safeguards for cross-border data transfers:
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderLi1: Standard Contract — we have adopted the Standard Contract for Outbound Cross-Border Transfer of Personal Information formulated by the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC), as our primary transfer mechanism
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderLi2: Personal Information Protection Impact Assessment (PIPIA) — we have conducted a PIPIA for cross-border transfers as required by PIPL Article 55, assessing the lawfulness and necessity of transfers, risks to your rights and interests, and protective measures
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderLi3: United Kingdom — Lumenshore Limited is based in the UK. Data is transferred under the Standard Contract with appropriate safeguards
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderLi4: United States (Vercel) — website hosting data is processed via Vercel's edge network under Standard Contractual Clauses
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderLi5: United States (Sentry) — error monitoring data (with your consent) is processed by Sentry under Standard Contractual Clauses
chinaPrivacy.crossBorderP2: Before any cross-border transfer, we inform you of the recipient's name, contact details, purpose, processing method, type of personal information, and how you may exercise your rights with the overseas recipient.
chinaPrivacy.rightsP1: As an individual whose personal information is processed under PIPL, you have the following rights:
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi1: Right to know and decide — you have the right to know about and make decisions regarding the processing of your personal information, and to restrict or refuse processing (except as provided by law)
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi2: Right to access and copy — you may request access to and copies of your personal information held by us
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi3: Right to portability — you may request transfer of your personal information to another handler you designate, where technically feasible
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi4: Right to correction and completion — you may request correction or completion of inaccurate or incomplete personal information
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi5: Right to deletion — you may request deletion of your personal information where the processing purpose has been achieved, the retention period has expired, you withdraw consent, or processing violates the law
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi6: Right to explanation — you may request an explanation of our personal information processing rules
chinaPrivacy.rightsLi7: Right to withdraw consent — where processing is based on your consent, you have the right to withdraw it at any time. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing before withdrawal
chinaPrivacy.rightsP2: We will respond to your requests within 15 days. To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>.
chinaPrivacy.dataLocalizationP1: PIPL and related regulations may require data localization for certain types of processing. Based on our current assessment, as a small-scale processor that does not meet the thresholds set by the CAC for mandatory data localization (processing personal information of more than one million individuals, or cumulatively transferring personal information of more than 100,000 individuals or sensitive personal information of more than 10,000 individuals abroad), we utilise the Standard Contract route for cross-border transfers rather than maintaining local data storage in China.
chinaPrivacy.cacP1: If you are unsatisfied with our handling of your personal information, you may lodge a complaint with the Cyberspace Administration of China or the relevant local department:

brazilPrivacy.heading: Brazil — Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD)
brazilPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo serves users globally, including in Brazil. As the largest market in Latin America, we comply with Brazil's Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD, Law No. 13,709/2018), in force since September 2020.
brazilPrivacy.applicabilityP1: The LGPD applies to any processing of personal data carried out in Brazil, of individuals located in Brazil, or where the data was collected in Brazil. Our multilingual content and newsletter/waitlist signups from Brazilian users bring our processing within the scope of the LGPD.
brazilPrivacy.legalBasesP1: The LGPD provides ten legal bases for processing personal data. We rely on the following:
brazilPrivacy.legalBasesLi1: Consent — we obtain your free, informed, and unambiguous consent for specific processing purposes (e.g., newsletter signup, analytics via Sentry)
brazilPrivacy.legalBasesLi2: Contract performance — processing necessary for the execution of the LumenLingo service agreement to which you are a party
brazilPrivacy.legalBasesLi3: Legitimate interests — processing necessary for our legitimate interests or those of third parties, except where your fundamental rights and freedoms prevail
brazilPrivacy.legalBasesLi4: Regulatory compliance — processing necessary to comply with our legal or regulatory obligations
brazilPrivacy.rightsP1: As a data subject under the LGPD, you have the following rights (LGPD Article 18):
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi1: Confirmation of processing — the right to confirm whether we process your personal data
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi2: Access — the right to access your personal data held by us
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi3: Correction — the right to correct incomplete, inaccurate, or outdated personal data
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi4: Anonymisation, blocking, or deletion — the right to request anonymisation, blocking, or deletion of unnecessary or excessive data, or data processed in non-compliance with the LGPD
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi5: Data portability — the right to portability of your personal data to another service or product provider
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi6: Deletion of data processed with consent — the right to request deletion of personal data processed on the basis of your consent
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi7: Information on shared data — the right to information about public and private entities with which we have shared your data
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi8: Information on consent denial — the right to information about the possibility and consequences of not providing consent
brazilPrivacy.rightsLi9: Revocation of consent — the right to revoke your consent at any time, via a free and easy procedure
brazilPrivacy.rightsP2: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>. We will respond without undue delay.
brazilPrivacy.crossBorderP1: Your personal data may be transferred outside Brazil. Under the LGPD, we ensure that international transfers are carried out with appropriate safeguards:
brazilPrivacy.crossBorderLi1: United Kingdom — Lumenshore Limited is based in the UK, which maintains data protection standards. We rely on standard contractual clauses as the transfer mechanism
brazilPrivacy.crossBorderLi2: United States (Vercel, Sentry) — website hosting and error monitoring data are processed in the US under standard contractual clauses
brazilPrivacy.crossBorderP2: The ANPD (Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados) may issue adequacy determinations for specific countries. We monitor ANPD guidance and update our transfer mechanisms accordingly.
brazilPrivacy.anpdHeading: ANPD — Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados
brazilPrivacy.anpdP1: If you are unsatisfied with our handling of your personal data, you may lodge a complaint with Brazil's National Data Protection Authority (ANPD):

germanyPrivacy.heading: Germany — BDSG & TTDSG
germanyPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo actively targets German-speaking users through our German (de) locale and multiple German language pairs. We comply with the Bundesdatenschutzgesetz (BDSG) and the Telekommunikation-Telemedien-Datenschutz-Gesetz (TTDSG), which supplement the GDPR in Germany.
germanyPrivacy.ttdsgP1: Under TTDSG §25, storing or accessing information on your end device requires your prior consent, unless strictly necessary for the service you requested. We implement this as follows:
germanyPrivacy.ttdsgLi1: Sentry error monitoring — activated only after you provide explicit consent via our cookie banner. No tracking occurs before consent
germanyPrivacy.ttdsgLi2: Essential storage — language preferences and cookie consent state are stored under the strictly necessary exemption
germanyPrivacy.ttdsgLi3: No dark patterns — our consent mechanism provides equal prominence to accept and refuse options, with no nudging or cookie walls
germanyPrivacy.ageP1: Germany has set the age of digital consent at 16 years under its GDPR implementation (BDSG). For users under 16, processing based on consent requires verifiable parental authorisation. We apply this threshold for users accessing our service through the German locale.
germanyPrivacy.dpoP1: Under BDSG §38, organisations where at least 20 persons are regularly engaged in the automated processing of personal data must appoint a Data Protection Officer. Based on our current assessment, Lumenshore Limited does not meet this threshold. We reassess this requirement periodically as our team grows.
germanyPrivacy.impressumP1: In compliance with the German Telemediengesetz (TMG) §5, our full legal imprint (Impressum) is available at <impressumLink>Impressum</impressumLink>. The Impressum contains our company details, registration information, and contact details as required by German law.

francePrivacy.heading: France — CNIL & French Data Protection Law
francePrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides a French (fr) locale and multiple French language pairs. We comply with the French data protection framework, including CNIL (Commission Nationale de l’Informatique et des Libertés) guidelines and the Loi Informatique et Libertés.
francePrivacy.cnilCookieP1: France enforces strict cookie consent rules under CNIL guidance. We implement these as follows:
francePrivacy.cnilCookieLi1: Equal prominence — our cookie banner provides equally prominent “Accept All” and “Refuse All” buttons, with no pre-checked boxes or nudging
francePrivacy.cnilCookieLi2: No implied consent — continued browsing does not constitute consent. Only explicit, affirmative action counts
francePrivacy.cnilCookieLi3: No cookie wall — access to LumenLingo is not conditioned on accepting non-essential cookies
francePrivacy.cnilCookieLi4: Purpose-specific consent — consent is granular per purpose, not bundled into a single acceptance
francePrivacy.cnilCookieLi5: Consent records — we maintain proof of consent as required by CNIL enforcement guidance
francePrivacy.ageP1: France has set the age of digital consent at 15 years. For users under 15, consent-based processing requires verifiable parental authorisation. We apply this threshold for users accessing our service through the French locale.
francePrivacy.postMortemP1: Under French law (Loi Informatique et Libertés, Article 85), you have the right to define instructions regarding the retention, deletion, and communication of your personal data after your death. To set post-mortem instructions, please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>.
francePrivacy.consumerP1: Under the Code de la consommation (Articles L221-1 to L221-18), consumers in France benefit from a 14-day withdrawal right for distance contracts. Pre-contractual information is provided in French through our fr locale.
francePrivacy.mentionsP1: In compliance with the French LCEN (Loi pour la Confiance dans l’Économie Numérique, Article 6), our full legal notices (Mentions Légales) are available at <mentionsLink>Mentions Légales</mentionsLink>.

polandPrivacy.heading: Poland — PUODO & Polish Regulatory Requirements
polandPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides a Polish (pl) locale and eight Polish language pairs. We comply with Polish data protection requirements, including PUODO (Prezes Urzędu Ochrony Danych Osobowych) enforcement guidance and the Polish implementation of the GDPR.
polandPrivacy.puodoP1: Poland applies the GDPR through national implementing legislation. PUODO, the Polish Data Protection Authority, actively enforces data protection requirements. Our Records of Processing Activities (ROPA) explicitly covers Polish users, and our data processing practices comply with PUODO enforcement guidance.
polandPrivacy.languageP1: In accordance with the Polish Consumer Rights Act (Ustawa o prawach konsumenta), our privacy policy, terms of service, and cookie policy are available in Polish through the pl locale. All pre-contractual information, including subscription disclosures, is provided in Polish for Polish consumers.
polandPrivacy.ageP1: Poland has set the age of digital consent at 16 years. For users under 16, consent-based processing requires verifiable parental authorisation. We apply this threshold for users accessing our service through the Polish locale.
polandPrivacy.ecommerceP1: Under the Act on Providing Services by Electronic Means (Ustawa o świadczeniu usług drogą elektroniczną), we provide clear service provider identification and allow complaints via electronic means. Our complaint handling procedure is accessible through the contact methods listed in our privacy policy.
polandPrivacy.telecomP1: Under Article 173 of the Polish Telecommunications Law (Prawo telekomunikacyjne), consent is required for storing data on end-user devices. Our cookie consent mechanism satisfies this requirement alongside ePrivacy/PECR compliance.
polandPrivacy.uokikP1: We comply with the requirements of UOKiK (Urząd Ochrony Konkurencji i Konsumentów). Our subscription terms, including auto-renewal and cancellation conditions, are presented clearly and fairly in Polish. We do not employ unfair contract terms or misleading practices.

ukrainePrivacy.heading: Ukraine — Data Protection Law & Regulatory Requirements
ukrainePrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides a Ukrainian (uk) locale and three Ukrainian language pairs (UA↔EN, UA↔PL, PL→UA). We comply with Ukrainian data protection law and respect the rights of Ukrainian users.
ukrainePrivacy.lawP1: Ukraine’s Law on Protection of Personal Data (Law No. 2297-VI) governs the processing of personal data of Ukrainian citizens. As we provide a Ukrainian locale and Ukrainian language pairs, we recognise our obligations under this law. Ukraine is currently aligning its data protection framework with the GDPR as part of its EU accession process.
ukrainePrivacy.rightsP1: Under Ukrainian law, data subjects have the right to access, correct, and request destruction of their personal data. These rights are consistent with and complementary to the rights described in the main GDPR provisions of this policy. Requests can be made through the contact methods listed below.
ukrainePrivacy.transferP1: Personal data is transferred outside Ukraine only to countries providing adequate protection, or on the basis of the data subject’s consent. Where required, we implement appropriate safeguards to ensure the security of transferred data.
ukrainePrivacy.languageP1: Our privacy policy, terms of service, and cookie policy are available in Ukrainian through the uk locale, ensuring Ukrainian consumers can review all legal disclosures in their language.
ukrainePrivacy.sanctionsP1: We have verified that there are no UK, EU, or US sanctions restricting the provision of consumer language-learning services to Ukrainian users. All in-app purchases are processed through Apple’s App Store infrastructure.
ukrainePrivacy.monitoringP1: We monitor ongoing Ukrainian regulatory developments, particularly as Ukraine aligns its data protection legislation with the EU acquis as part of the EU accession process.

middleEastPrivacy.heading: Middle East — UAE, Saudi Arabia & Bahrain Data Protection
middleEastPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides an Arabic (ar) locale and Arabic language pairs (EN↔AR, PL→AR). We comply with data protection laws applicable in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Bahrain.
middleEastPrivacy.uaeP1: The UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 (Personal Data Protection Law), effective since January 2022 with implementing regulations issued in 2023, applies to the processing of personal data of UAE residents. We comply with its requirements for consent, purpose limitation, data minimisation, and transparency. Our privacy policy discloses processing details as required by this law.
middleEastPrivacy.uaeRightsP1: Under the UAE PDPL, data subjects have the right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, and objection. Cross-border data transfers are permitted to countries with adequate protection or with appropriate safeguards in place.
middleEastPrivacy.saudiHeading: Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law
middleEastPrivacy.saudiP1: The Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL), effective since September 2023, applies to the processing of personal data of Saudi residents. We obtain explicit consent for processing where required and comply with purpose limitation requirements. Cross-border transfers are conducted in accordance with the PDPL’s requirements.
middleEastPrivacy.bahrainP1: Bahrain’s Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL, Law No. 30 of 2018) provides a GDPR-like framework for data protection. We recognise the rights of Bahraini users who access our service and apply consistent data protection standards.
middleEastPrivacy.arabicP1: Our privacy policy, terms of service, and cookie policy are available in Arabic through the ar locale, with proper right-to-left (RTL) layout for all legal content.
middleEastPrivacy.culturalP1: We review our learning content, including flashcard examples and cultural references, to ensure cultural appropriateness for Middle Eastern users.
middleEastPrivacy.complianceP1: We prioritise compliance with UAE and Saudi Arabia data protection requirements as the largest Arabic-speaking markets, while monitoring regulatory developments in Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Jordan, and Egypt.

spainLatamPrivacy.heading: Spain & Latin America — LOPDGDD & Regional Laws
spainLatamPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides a Spanish (es) locale and multiple Spanish language pairs (EN↔ES, PL→ES, DE→ES). We comply with Spain’s LOPDGDD and monitor data protection developments across Latin America.
spainLatamPrivacy.spainP1: Spain’s Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos y Garantía de Derechos Digitales (LOPDGDD) implements the GDPR with additional digital rights. The Spanish age of digital consent is 14 years. In addition to standard GDPR rights, Spanish users enjoy the right to digital disconnect, the right to digital education, and the right to rectification on the internet.
spainLatamPrivacy.spainP2: If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you may file a complaint with the AEPD (Agencia Española de Protección de Datos) at www.aepd.es.
spainLatamPrivacy.argentinaP1: Argentina’s Ley de Protección de Datos Personales (25.326) provides a framework similar to GDPR. Argentina holds an EU adequacy decision, facilitating data transfers. We monitor our Argentine user base and assess compliance requirements accordingly.
spainLatamPrivacy.mexicoP1: Mexico’s Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales requires a privacy notice (Aviso de Privacidad) before collecting personal data. This notice includes controller identity, purposes of processing, data transfers, and mechanisms for exercising rights.
spainLatamPrivacy.colombiaP1: Colombia’s Ley 1581 de 2012 requires prior authorisation (consent) for data processing. Database registration with the SIC (Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio) may apply if significant Colombian user activity develops.
spainLatamPrivacy.languageP1: Our privacy policy, terms of service, and cookie policy are available in Spanish through the es locale. The Spanish version includes LOPDGDD-specific digital rights and AEPD complaint information.
spainLatamPrivacy.complianceP1: Tier 1: Spain (EU/GDPR + LOPDGDD) receives priority compliance attention. Tier 2: Mexico and Argentina are monitored for significant user base development. Tier 3: Other Latin American countries (Chile, Peru, Ecuador, etc.) are monitored as their data protection frameworks evolve.

canadaPrivacy.heading: Canada — PIPEDA & Quebec Law 25
canadaPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides English (en) and French (fr) locales and EN↔FR language pairs, serving Canadian English and French speakers. We comply with Canada’s Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and Quebec’s Law 25.
canadaPrivacy.pipedaP1: PIPEDA applies to private-sector organisations that collect, use, or disclose personal information in the course of commercial activity. Our website is available in English and French, and we collect personal information through newsletter and waitlist signups from Canadian users.
canadaPrivacy.principlesP1: We adhere to PIPEDA’s 10 Fair Information Principles: Accountability, Identifying Purposes, Consent, Limiting Collection, Limiting Use/Disclosure/Retention, Accuracy, Safeguards, Openness, Individual Access, and Challenging Compliance. Our privacy practices are designed to satisfy each principle.
canadaPrivacy.breachP1: Since November 2018, PIPEDA requires mandatory breach notification. We report breaches creating a “real risk of significant harm” to the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC), notify affected individuals, and maintain breach records for two years.
canadaPrivacy.quebecP1: Quebec’s Law 25 modernises data protection with GDPR-like requirements including Privacy Impact Assessments, consent granularity, and enhanced individual rights. As our service is available in French and serves Quebec users, we comply with these additional requirements.
canadaPrivacy.disclosuresP1: Canadian users may file a complaint with the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC) at www.priv.gc.ca. Quebec residents may also contact the Commission d’accès à l’information du Québec (CAI) at www.cai.gouv.qc.ca.

southKoreaPrivacy.heading: South Korea — PIPA Awareness
southKoreaPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo does not currently provide a Korean (ko) locale or Korean language pairs. However, Korean users may access our service via the English locale.
southKoreaPrivacy.assessmentP1: South Korea’s Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA) is one of the world’s strictest data protection regimes. PIPA applies to “personal information handlers” processing personal information of Korean residents. Currently, the absence of a Korean locale represents lower risk, but PIPA may apply if Korean users sign up via the English locale.
southKoreaPrivacy.requirementsP1: PIPA requires purpose-specific consent (not bundled), separate consent for cross-border transfers, mandatory appointment of a Chief Privacy Officer, breach notification to PIPC and affected individuals without delay, and rights of access, correction, deletion, and suspension of processing.
southKoreaPrivacy.monitoringP1: We monitor Korean user metrics. If a material Korean user base develops, we will conduct a full PIPA compliance review including Korea Communications Commission (KCC) requirements for online service providers.

australiaPrivacy.heading: Australia — Privacy Act 1988
australiaPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo’s English (en) locale serves Australian users. We comply with Australia’s Privacy Act 1988, the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs), and the Notifiable Data Breaches (NDB) scheme.
australiaPrivacy.actP1: The Privacy Act 1988 regulates the handling of personal information by Australian Government agencies and private sector organisations with an annual turnover of more than AUD 3 million, or those that trade in personal information. As a UK-based service accessible to Australian users, we voluntarily align with APP requirements.
australiaPrivacy.appsP1: The 13 APPs govern the collection, use, disclosure, quality, security, access, and correction of personal information. We collect only the personal information reasonably necessary for our language-learning service, provide transparent notice, and allow users to access and correct their data.
australiaPrivacy.ndbP1: The NDB scheme requires notification to the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) and affected individuals when a data breach is likely to result in serious harm. We maintain breach detection and response procedures aligned with NDB requirements.
australiaPrivacy.aclP1: Under Australian Consumer Law (ACL), goods and services come with consumer guarantees that cannot be excluded. Nothing in our terms excludes, restricts, or modifies consumer guarantees under the ACL.
australiaPrivacy.disclosuresP1: Australian users may lodge a complaint with the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) at www.oaic.gov.au. We respond to OAIC enquiries and investigations in accordance with the Privacy Act.

indiaPrivacy.heading: India — DPDPA 2023
indiaPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo does not currently provide a Hindi (hi) locale, but Indian users may access our service via the English locale. We are preparing for compliance with India’s Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDPA).
indiaPrivacy.applicabilityP1: The DPDPA applies to processing of digital personal data within India, as well as processing outside India if it relates to offering goods or services to individuals in India. As our English locale is accessible from India, we monitor DPDPA developments and align our practices accordingly.
