indiaPrivacy.requirementsP1: The DPDPA requires lawful purpose and consent as grounds for processing, purpose limitation, data minimisation, accuracy obligations, and storage limitation. Data fiduciaries must implement reasonable security safeguards and respond to data principal rights including correction, erasure, and nomination of representatives.
indiaPrivacy.crossBorderP1: The DPDPA permits cross-border transfers except to countries specifically restricted by the Indian government. We process data in the UK and EU, which are not currently restricted jurisdictions.
indiaPrivacy.childrenP1: Under the DPDPA, the age of consent for data processing is 18 years. Processing children’s data requires verifiable consent from a parent or lawful guardian. We do not knowingly collect data from individuals under 18 without parental consent.
indiaPrivacy.monitoringP1: The DPDPA’s implementing rules are still being finalised. We monitor developments from the Data Protection Board of India (DPBI) and will update our practices as rules are published.

switzerlandPrivacy.heading: Switzerland — nFADP (Revised Federal Act on Data Protection)
switzerlandPrivacy.intro: LumenLingo provides German (de) and French (fr) locales serving Swiss users. We comply with Switzerland’s revised Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP / Bundesgesetz über den Datenschutz), which entered into force on 1 September 2023.
switzerlandPrivacy.applicabilityP1: The nFADP applies to private persons and federal bodies processing personal data of individuals in Switzerland, with extraterritorial scope for foreign controllers whose processing produces effects in Switzerland. As we provide German and French locales with DE/FR language pairs deliberately targeting Swiss users, and collect personal data through newsletter and waitlist signups, the nFADP applies to our processing.
switzerlandPrivacy.obligationsP1: Under the nFADP, we fulfil our information duty (Art. 19–21) by informing data subjects about our identity, processing purposes, recipients, cross-border transfers, retention periods, and data subject rights. Consent must be informed, voluntary, and given for specific processing; silence does not constitute consent. Processing of sensitive data requires explicit consent.
switzerlandPrivacy.crossBorderP1: We transfer data only to countries with adequate protection per the FDPIC’s adequacy list, or with appropriate safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) with Swiss addenda. Switzerland recognises UK adequacy. For transfers to US-based sub-processors (Vercel, Sentry), we rely on the Swiss-US Data Privacy Framework and SCCs with Swiss addenda.
switzerlandPrivacy.rightsP1: Swiss residents enjoy the right of access (Art. 25–27, response within 30 days, free of charge), data portability in common electronic format (Art. 28–29), rectification (Art. 32(1)), deletion or destruction (Art. 32(2)(c)), and the right to object to processing. Note that the nFADP does not include a right to restrict processing as under the GDPR.
switzerlandPrivacy.representativeP1: Under Art. 14–15, controllers outside Switzerland may need to designate a Swiss representative if they regularly process data of Swiss residents on a large scale with high risk. At our current scale, we do not meet these thresholds. We will reassess as our Swiss user base grows and appoint a representative if required.
switzerlandPrivacy.breachP1: Under Art. 24, we notify the Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC) as soon as possible for data breaches likely to result in high risk to data subjects. We also notify affected individuals when necessary for their protection.
switzerlandPrivacy.fdpicP1: Swiss residents may lodge a complaint with the Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC) at www.edoeb.admin.ch.
switzerlandPrivacy.penaltiesP1: The nFADP uniquely imposes criminal fines of up to CHF 250,000 on individuals (not just companies) for intentional breaches of information duties, cross-border transfer rules, or representative requirements. Directors and data protection officers are personally liable. Lumenshore’s directors are aware of these obligations.

ageConsent.heading: Age of Digital Consent — Multi-Jurisdiction
ageConsent.intro: The age at which a person can give valid consent for data processing varies by jurisdiction. As a language-learning service likely used by younger learners, we apply locale-aware age requirements.
ageConsent.mappingIntro: The following table summarises the minimum digital consent age in each jurisdiction where we operate:
ageConsent.mappingChina: China: 14 (PIPL Art. 31 — under-14 classified as sensitive personal information)
ageConsent.mappingSouthKorea: South Korea: 14 (PIPA — parental consent for under-14)
ageConsent.mappingJapan: Japan: 16 (APPI — case-by-case for minors; we apply 16 as a conservative default)
ageConsent.mappingUae: UAE: 18 (UAE PDPL — minors require guardian consent)
ageConsent.mappingSaudi: Saudi Arabia: 18 (PDPL — minors require guardian consent)
ageConsent.mappingIndia: India: 18 (DPDPA 2023 — children defined as under 18; verifiable parental consent required)
ageConsent.mappingBrazil: Brazil: 12/18 (LGPD Art. 14 — children under 12 require parental consent; 12–17 processed in their best interest)
ageConsent.mappingAustralia: Australia: capacity-based (Privacy Act 1988 — no fixed age; assessed by individual capacity)
ageConsent.approachP1: We apply locale-aware age verification. When you interact with our website forms (newsletter, waitlist), the minimum age displayed and enforced matches your detected locale. For locales not explicitly mapped, we default to 16 — the highest common EU digital consent age — ensuring compliance across all jurisdictions.
ageConsent.approachP2: Our iOS app does not require account registration and stores data locally on the device. No personal information is transmitted to us from app users, so age verification is not required for app use.
ageConsent.parentalFlowP1: If you are between 13 and your jurisdiction’s consent age, you cannot submit personal information through our website without parental consent. Our forms require a self-declaration confirming you meet the age threshold for your jurisdiction.
ageConsent.parentalFlowP2: For users under the applicable age: we display an age-appropriate notice explaining our data collection practices. Essential service functionality (such as browsing the website) remains available without consent-based data collection.
ageConsent.chinaHeading: China PIPL — Special Protections for Under-14
ageConsent.chinaP1: Under China’s Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL Art. 31), personal information of individuals under 14 is classified as “sensitive personal information.” Processing requires separate consent from a parent or guardian, and we must conduct a Personal Information Protection Impact Assessment for any such processing.
ageConsent.chinaP2: We do not knowingly collect personal information from Chinese users under 14. If we discover such data has been collected, we will delete it promptly and notify the parent or guardian.

enhancedChildPrivacy.heading: Children’s Enhanced Privacy Protections
enhancedChildPrivacy.intro: As a language-learning service likely used by younger learners, we apply enhanced privacy protections for users under 18. These protections exceed baseline regulatory requirements and reflect our commitment to child safety.
enhancedChildPrivacy.minimizationP1: We collect only data strictly necessary for service delivery from all users, with additional restrictions for verified minors:
enhancedChildPrivacy.minimizationLi1: No marketing communications are sent to verified minor users.
enhancedChildPrivacy.minimizationLi2: No session-replay or behavioural-analytics tracking is applied to minor users.
enhancedChildPrivacy.minimizationLi3: Analytics is limited to essential error monitoring to maintain service quality.
enhancedChildPrivacy.minimizationLi4: No third-party advertising trackers are loaded for minor users.
enhancedChildPrivacy.parentalP1: Where parental or guardian consent has been provided:
enhancedChildPrivacy.parentalLi1: Parents or guardians may review the data we hold about their child by emailing legal@lumenlingo.com.
enhancedChildPrivacy.parentalLi2: Parents or guardians may revoke consent at any time, and we will cease processing the child’s personal data within 30 days.
enhancedChildPrivacy.parentalLi3: Upon parental request, we will delete all of the minor’s personal data, subject to any legal retention obligations.
enhancedChildPrivacy.ageAppropriateP1: For users aged 13–17, we aim to provide privacy information that is:
enhancedChildPrivacy.ageAppropriateLi1: Written in clear, plain language appropriate for a younger audience.
enhancedChildPrivacy.ageAppropriateLi2: Concise and focused on what matters most: what data we collect, why, and how to control it.
enhancedChildPrivacy.ageAppropriateLi3: Presented in a visual, accessible format rather than dense legal text wherever possible.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeP1: The UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) Age Appropriate Design Code applies to online services likely to be accessed by children under 18. As a language-learning service, LumenLingo falls within scope. We have assessed our service against all 15 standards of the Code:
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi1: Best interests of the child: data practices prioritise children's wellbeing over commercial interests.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi2: Data protection impact assessments: we conduct DPIAs for features likely to be accessed by children.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi3: Age-appropriate application: we apply age-appropriate protections by default; where precise age is unknown, we treat all users as potentially under 18 for privacy purposes.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi4: Transparency: privacy information is provided in clear, age-appropriate language.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi5: Detrimental use of data: we do not use children's data in ways that have been shown to be detrimental to their wellbeing.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi6: Policies and community standards: our published terms uphold the Code's standards.
enhancedChildPrivacy.appStoreP1: LumenLingo is rated 4+ (educational content) on the Apple App Store. This rating reflects the app’s educational nature and absence of objectionable content. The app does not require account registration and stores all learning data locally on the device.
enhancedChildPrivacy.appStoreP2: For website services that collect personal data (newsletter, waitlist), the minimum age is enforced according to the user’s jurisdiction as described in our Age of Digital Consent section above.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi7: Default settings: high-privacy defaults are applied for all users, ensuring children receive maximum protection without needing to adjust settings.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi8: Data minimisation: only data essential for the learning service is processed.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi9: Data sharing: we do not share children's personal data with third parties for marketing or advertising purposes.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi10: Geolocation: we do not collect or use precise geolocation data.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi11: Parental controls: not applicable — LumenLingo does not currently provide parental control features. If introduced, they will be designed to provide an appropriate level of monitoring without excessive surveillance.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi12: Profiling: children are not profiled for marketing or behavioural targeting. Our spaced-repetition algorithm is purely pedagogical and does not constitute profiling under the Code.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi13: Nudge techniques: we do not use design techniques that encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data or weaken their privacy protections.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi14: Connected toys and devices: not applicable — LumenLingo is a software application and does not interact with connected toys or IoT devices.
enhancedChildPrivacy.childrensCodeLi15: Online tools: we provide accessible tools for users to exercise their data rights, including account deletion and data export.

euRepresentative.heading: EU Representative (GDPR Art. 27)
euRepresentative.assessment1: Lumenshore Limited is incorporated in England and Wales and is therefore established outside the European Economic Area (EEA). Where Lumenshore offers its Service to individuals in the EEA or monitors their behaviour within the EEA, it may be subject to the obligation under GDPR Art. 27 to designate a representative in the EU.
euRepresentative.assessment2: LumenLingo processes minimal personal data and does not systematically monitor the behaviour of EU-based individuals. The app's learning algorithms operate locally on users' devices and do not involve server-side profiling or tracking.
euRepresentative.assessment3: Lumenshore has assessed its processing activities and determined that, given the current scope and nature of data processing, the designation of an EU representative under Art. 27 is not currently mandatory. However, as a matter of good practice and transparency, Lumenshore has voluntarily designated the following contact point for EU data protection inquiries:
euRepresentative.contact1: For data protection inquiries from EU-based individuals or supervisory authorities:
euRepresentative.contact3: Lumenshore will review this assessment periodically and will formally appoint an EU representative under GDPR Art. 27 if required by changes in processing activities, applicable guidance, or regulatory requirements.
euRepresentative.supervisory1: EU-based individuals have the right to lodge a complaint with their local data protection supervisory authority. A list of EEA supervisory authorities is available on the European Data Protection Board website.
euRepresentative.supervisory2: If Lumenshore formally appoints an EU representative in the future, the representative's details will be published in this Privacy Policy and communicated to the relevant supervisory authority in accordance with GDPR Art. 27(4).

introduction.heading: Introduction
introduction.p1: Welcome to LumenLingo. These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern your use of the LumenLingo mobile application (“the App”) operated by Lumenshore Limited (Company No. 09607326), a company registered in England and Wales (“Lumenshore”, “we”, “us”, or “our”).
introduction.p2: By downloading, installing, or using LumenLingo, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you don't agree with any part of these Terms, please don't use the App.

acceptance.heading: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.intro: By accessing or using LumenLingo, you confirm that:
acceptance.li1: You are at least 13 years old (or the minimum age required in your jurisdiction).
acceptance.li2: If you are under 18, you have obtained consent from a parent or legal guardian.
acceptance.li3: You have the legal capacity to enter into a binding agreement.
acceptance.li4: You will comply with these Terms and all applicable laws.

yourAccount.intro: LumenLingo does not require traditional account registration. Your identity within the App is tied to your Apple ID and device. You are responsible for:
yourAccount.li1: Maintaining the security of your Apple ID and device access.
yourAccount.li2: All activity that occurs through your use of the App.
yourAccount.li3: Keeping your device's operating system updated for the best experience and security.

subscriptions.heading: Subscriptions & Billing
subscriptions.intro: LumenLingo offers free and premium membership tiers. Premium features require a paid subscription.
subscriptions.li1: Billing — all subscriptions are processed and managed by Apple through the App Store. We do not handle payments directly.
subscriptions.li2: Free tier — provides access to core learning features with limited language pairs and practice modes.
subscriptions.li3: Premium tiers — unlock additional language pairs, unlimited practice, soundscapes, breathing orbs, and other premium features as described in the App.
subscriptions.li4: Auto-renewal — subscriptions auto-renew unless cancelled at least 24 hours before the end of the current billing period.
subscriptions.li5: Cancellation — you can cancel your subscription at any time through your Apple ID Settings → Subscriptions. Cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period.
subscriptions.li6: Refunds — refund requests are handled by Apple in accordance with their refund policies. Contact Apple Support for refund enquiries.
subscriptions.li7: Price changes — we may adjust subscription prices. You will be notified in advance, and changes apply only to your next renewal period.

distanceSelling.li2: Service description — LumenLingo is a digital language-learning application offering flashcards, grammar exercises, word-building games, soundscapes, and visual themes across multiple language pairs.
distanceSelling.li3: Price & payment — subscription prices are displayed in the App and on our website, inclusive of applicable taxes. All payments are processed by Apple through the App Store. Lumenshore does not handle payment card details.
distanceSelling.li4: Contract duration — subscriptions are monthly rolling contracts with no minimum commitment. They auto-renew unless cancelled at least 24 hours before the end of the current billing period.
distanceSelling.li5: Cooling-off period — under the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013, you have a 14-day right to cancel distance contracts without giving any reason. When you initiate a purchase through the App Store, Apple presents the subscription terms including the start date and auto-renewal terms. By confirming the purchase (via Face ID, Touch ID, or passcode), you expressly consent to the immediate supply of digital content and acknowledge that you lose your 14-day cancellation right once the digital content is made available to you.
distanceSelling.li6: Cancellation — you may cancel future renewals at any time via Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period. You retain access to premium features until that date.
distanceSelling.li7: Refunds — all refund requests are handled by Apple. To request a refund, visit reportaproblem.apple.com. Lumenshore does not process refunds directly, as Apple is the merchant of record for all App Store purchases.
distanceSelling.li8: Complaints — if you have a complaint about our service, please contact us at legal@lumenlingo.com. We aim to resolve all complaints within 14 days.
distanceSelling.li5a: Consent confirmation — we will send a confirmation of your consent to immediate performance via the App Store receipt delivered to your Apple ID email address, which constitutes a durable medium under CCR 2013 reg. 16.

vatCompliance.heading: VAT & Tax Treatment
vatCompliance.intro: Lumenshore Limited is registered for UK Value Added Tax (VAT No. GB 270411929). This section explains how VAT applies to our services.
vatCompliance.iapP1: All in-app purchases made through Apple’s App Store are processed by Apple as the merchant of record. Under the UK Finance Act 2021, Apple is deemed the supplier for VAT purposes and collects and remits VAT to HMRC on all iOS in-app purchases made by UK consumers.
vatCompliance.iapP2: Prices displayed in the App Store include any applicable VAT. The final price charged is determined by Apple based on your App Store region and local tax rules.
vatCompliance.directP1: All prices displayed on our website for UK and EU consumers include VAT at the applicable rate. For UK consumers, the standard VAT rate of 20% applies to digital services.
vatCompliance.directP2: Prices charged via Apple’s App Store may vary by location. The price shown on our website is indicative; the final price is always as displayed in the App Store at the time of purchase.
vatCompliance.recordsP1: We retain all VAT and financial records for a minimum of 6 years in accordance with HMRC requirements. Our VAT returns are filed digitally via Making Tax Digital (MTD) compatible software as required by UK law.
vatCompliance.disclaimerAppStore: Subscriptions are billed through Apple’s App Store. Prices include applicable taxes. Final price may vary by region.

euVatCompliance.heading: EU VAT on Digital Services
euVatCompliance.intro: As a UK-based provider of digital services to EU consumers, we comply with EU VAT rules for cross-border digital supplies.
euVatCompliance.appleMarketplaceP1: Under EU marketplace rules and DAC7, Apple is deemed the supplier for VAT purposes for all in-app purchases made through the App Store by EU consumers. Apple collects and remits VAT at each EU member state’s rate (e.g. Germany 19%, France 20%, Spain 21%, Poland 23%, Italy 22%).
euVatCompliance.appleMarketplaceP2: Lumenshore’s obligation is limited to proper revenue recognition of the net amount received from Apple after deduction of commission and VAT.
euVatCompliance.ossP1: For any digital services sold directly to EU consumers outside the App Store, we use the UK Non-Union One Stop Shop (OSS) scheme administered by HMRC. This allows us to account for EU VAT through a single quarterly return rather than registering in each member state individually.
euVatCompliance.ossP2: EU VAT is applied at each member state’s rate and remitted through HMRC to the relevant tax authorities. Records of all EU supplies are retained for a minimum of 10 years.
euVatCompliance.b2bP1: Where digital services are supplied to EU businesses, the reverse charge mechanism applies. The EU business customer self-accounts for VAT in their own country. We verify EU VAT registration numbers via the VIES system before zero-rating any invoice.
euVatCompliance.evidenceP1: We comply with the EU two-piece-of-evidence rule, which requires at least two non-contradictory pieces of evidence of customer location (such as IP address and billing address). For App Store purchases, Apple provides this evidence automatically.

internationalTax.heading: International Tax Obligations
internationalTax.intro: As a global service provider, Lumenshore monitors tax obligations across all jurisdictions where our app is available. In most markets, Apple handles tax collection as the marketplace operator.
internationalTax.japanP1: Japan’s Consumption Tax (JCT) of 10% applies to digital services. Apple, as a registered foreign business in Japan, handles JCT collection and remittance for all iOS in-app purchases. Direct sales to Japanese consumers exceeding ¥10 million annually would require separate JCT registration.
internationalTax.usP1: There is no federal digital services tax in the US. State-level sales tax varies and not all states tax digital services. Apple handles sales tax collection for in-app purchases across all US states in accordance with each state’s economic nexus rules.
internationalTax.australiaP1: Australia’s Goods and Services Tax (GST) of 10% applies to digital products supplied to Australian consumers. Apple handles GST collection for App Store purchases. Direct sales exceeding AUD 75,000 annually would require separate GST registration.
internationalTax.indiaP1: India’s GST of 18% applies to online information and database access services. Apple handles GST for in-app purchases. Non-resident providers may need separate GST registration for direct sales to Indian consumers.
internationalTax.oecdP1: The OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax of 15% currently applies to multinational enterprises with consolidated revenue exceeding €750 million. This threshold does not currently apply to Lumenshore, but we monitor developments for potential future changes.
internationalTax.corporationTaxP1: Lumenshore pays UK Corporation Tax on worldwide profits at the applicable rate (currently 25% for profits over £250,000, or the small profits rate of 19%). We assess eligibility for R&D Tax Credits for qualifying software development expenditure and Creative Industry Tax Relief where applicable.
internationalTax.transferPricingP1: As a single-entity company, Lumenshore does not currently have intercompany transactions requiring transfer pricing documentation. Should the corporate structure change, we will comply with OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines and UK legislation.

pricingCompliance.heading: Pricing Display Compliance
pricingCompliance.intro: Lumenshore is committed to transparent, accurate pricing across all platforms. This section details our compliance with pricing display regulations in every jurisdiction where our app is available.
pricingCompliance.ukPriceMarkingP1: All prices displayed to UK consumers on our website include VAT at the applicable rate. Prices shown represent the total amount payable with no hidden charges. Where a range of pricing tiers is offered, pricing starts from the lowest available tier (Free, £0.00).
pricingCompliance.euConsumerRightsP1: Prices are displayed in a clear and comprehensible manner. The total price including all applicable taxes is shown before any purchase commitment. Subscription billing frequency (monthly) is clearly indicated alongside the price. No additional delivery or postal charges apply to our digital service.
pricingCompliance.euOmnibusP1: In compliance with the Price Indication Directive 98/6/EC as amended by the Omnibus Directive, any promotional or reduced pricing on our website will display the previous lowest price charged during the 30 days prior to the reduction. Our current pricing tiers are standard ongoing prices and are not presented as time-limited discounts.
pricingCompliance.iosAppPricingP1: In-app purchases use Apple StoreKit to display localised prices that include local taxes automatically. The price shown in the app always matches the price Apple will charge. Regional pricing follows Apple’s App Store Connect tier configuration, ensuring consistency between displayed and charged amounts.
pricingCompliance.websitePricingP1: Our website displays prices in GBP (British Pounds Sterling) inclusive of UK VAT. When purchasing via the iOS app, the final price is determined by Apple’s App Store based on your location and local tax rules. We do not display misleading discount claims or artificial price reductions.

sanctionsCompliance.heading: Sanctions & Trade Compliance
sanctionsCompliance.intro: Lumenshore Limited is a UK-incorporated company (Company No. 09607326) that distributes LumenLingo globally via the Apple App Store. We maintain compliance with applicable sanctions regimes across all jurisdictions in which we operate.
sanctionsCompliance.ukP1: We comply with the Sanctions and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2018 and regulations administered by the Office of Financial Sanctions Implementation (OFSI). As a consumer language-learning application, LumenLingo is classified as non-strategic software and is generally exempt from most trade restrictions. We review OFSI consolidated sanctions lists and do not knowingly provide services to sanctioned individuals or entities.
sanctionsCompliance.usP1: Because LumenLingo is distributed via the Apple App Store, which operates from the United States, US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions apply to distribution. Apple already restricts App Store access in comprehensively sanctioned countries including Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria, and the Crimea, Donetsk, and Luhansk regions. Lumenshore does not independently distribute to any sanctioned territory.
sanctionsCompliance.euP1: We monitor EU sanctions lists for changes that may affect the provision of digital services, particularly in regions relevant to our Arabic-speaking and Ukrainian user base. Apple’s App Store distribution restrictions align with EU sanctions requirements. We do not provide services to EU-sanctioned individuals or entities.
sanctionsCompliance.ukraineP1: Ukraine is not subject to UK, EU, or US sanctions. Services to Ukrainian users are lawful and actively supported. LumenLingo provides a Ukrainian locale and three Ukrainian language pairs (UA↔EN, UA↔PL, PL→UA). However, the Crimea, Donetsk, and Luhansk regions have restricted status under multiple sanctions regimes, and Apple restricts App Store access in these regions.
sanctionsCompliance.screeningP1: For account-bearing users who make purchases, sanctions screening is handled at the payment infrastructure level by Apple’s App Store. Apple performs Know Your Customer (KYC) checks on Apple ID accounts and restricts access from sanctioned territories. LumenLingo does not process payments directly and relies on Apple’s compliance infrastructure for transaction-level screening.

encryptionExport.heading: Encryption & Export Controls
encryptionExport.intro: LumenLingo uses industry-standard encryption technologies provided by the Apple iOS platform. This section documents our encryption use for transparency and regulatory compliance.
encryptionExport.auditP1: The following encryption technologies are used within LumenLingo, all of which are provided by the Apple iOS platform:
encryptionExport.auditLi1: HTTPS/TLS — All network communications use Transport Layer Security for data in transit
encryptionExport.auditLi2: iCloud Encryption — Data synced via iCloud uses Apple's end-to-end encryption
encryptionExport.auditLi3: SwiftData File Protection — Local data at rest is protected by iOS file-level encryption
encryptionExport.auditLi4: Keychain Services — Sensitive credentials are stored in the iOS Keychain with hardware-backed encryption
encryptionExport.earP1: LumenLingo uses only standard, system-provided encryption (HTTPS/TLS, iCloud, iOS file protection). These qualify for the EAR License Exception ENC (Section 740.17) as mass-market encryption software using published cryptographic standards. No proprietary or custom encryption algorithms are implemented.
encryptionExport.frenchP1: France requires declaration for the import of encryption software under ANSSI regulations. Applications using only standard HTTPS/TLS communications and system-provided encryption are generally exempt from declaration requirements. LumenLingo uses exclusively Apple platform-provided encryption and does not implement any custom cryptographic functionality.
encryptionExport.appStoreP1: LumenLingo's export compliance is declared through Apple's App Store Connect. The app uses encryption (HTTPS, iCloud) but qualifies for exemption as it relies exclusively on system-provided encryption technologies.

ukAdr.heading: UK Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR)
ukAdr.p1: Under the Alternative Dispute Resolution for Consumer Disputes (Competent Authorities and Information) Regulations 2015, we are required to inform you about alternative dispute resolution.
ukAdr.p2: Lumenshore Limited is willing to participate in alternative dispute resolution through a certified ADR body. The relevant ADR provider is:
ukAdr.providerAddress: Address: 100 St Paul's Churchyard, London, EC4M 8BU
ukAdr.p3: Before referring a matter to ADR, we ask that you first contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink> so that we can attempt to resolve your complaint directly.
ukAdr.p4: If you are an EU consumer, you may also use the European Commission's Online Dispute Resolution (ODR) platform at <odrLink>https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/</odrLink>.

systemRequirements.heading: System Requirements
systemRequirements.p1: To use the LumenLingo iOS app, your device must meet the following minimum requirements:
systemRequirements.li3: Internet connectivity: an active internet connection is required for initial setup, content downloads, authentication, and subscription management. Certain features (flashcard review of previously downloaded content) may be available offline.
systemRequirements.li4: Storage: approximately 100 MB of free storage space for the app, plus additional space for downloaded content
systemRequirements.li5: iCloud: an iCloud account is required for cross-device sync of learning progress. iCloud sync is optional; the app is fully functional without it on a single device.
systemRequirements.p2: To access the LumenLingo website, you need a modern web browser with JavaScript enabled (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, or Edge — current version or one version prior).

appleLicensedEula.heading: Apple Licensed Application EULA
appleLicensedEula.p1: To the extent required by Apple’s App Store terms, this agreement incorporates by reference Apple’s Licensed Application End User License Agreement, available at <appleEulaLink>https://www.apple.com/legal/internet-services/itunes/dev/stdeula/</appleEulaLink>.
appleLicensedEula.p2: In the event of a conflict between these Terms and Apple’s Licensed Application EULA, Apple’s EULA shall prevail to the extent of the conflict, but only in respect of matters within Apple’s EULA’s scope.

accessibility.heading: Accessibility
accessibility.p1: We are committed to making LumenLingo accessible to all users. We strive to comply with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA for our website and follow Apple’s Human Interface Guidelines for accessibility in our iOS app. For details, please see our <accessibilityLink>Accessibility Statement</accessibilityLink>.
accessibility.p2: If you encounter any accessibility barriers, please contact us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink>. We recognise our obligations under the Equality Act 2010 to make reasonable adjustments and will endeavour to address any reported issues promptly.

CookieConsent.bannerDescription: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience, monitor errors, and understand site usage. You choose what you're comfortable with.
CookieConsent.preferencesDescription: Choose which categories of cookies you'd like to allow. Essential cookies are always active as they're needed for the site to work properly.

essential.label: Essential
essential.description: Required for the website to function. Includes language preferences and consent state. Cannot be disabled.

analytics.label: Analytics
analytics.description: Anonymous, cookie-free page view analytics via Vercel Analytics. Helps us understand which pages are most useful.

errorMonitoring.label: Error Monitoring
errorMonitoring.description: Error reporting via Sentry so we can fix issues quickly. No personal data is captured.

sessionReplay.label: Session Replay
sessionReplay.description: Records anonymised browsing sessions when errors occur to help us reproduce and fix bugs. All text and media are masked.

CookieConsent.gpcNotice: Your browser’s Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal is active. Non-essential tracking has been automatically disabled to respect your privacy preference. To enable these categories, disable GPC in your browser settings first.

meta.title: Cookie Policy — LumenLingo
meta.description: Learn how LumenLingo uses cookies, localStorage, and similar technologies. Review storage mechanisms, durations, and manage your preferences.
meta.ogDescription: Full transparency on cookies, local storage, and caching used by LumenLingo. Manage your preferences at any time.

hero.heading: Cookie Policy
hero.subtitle: We believe in full transparency. Here's exactly what data our website stores on your device and why.

overview.heading: Overview
overview.p1: LumenLingo uses minimal client-side storage to keep the site working smoothly. We do not use traditional tracking cookies. Instead, we rely on localStorage, sessionStorage, and a Service Worker cache — each serving a specific, documented purpose.
overview.p2: You can manage your preferences at any time using the Cookie Settings button in the footer, or by clearing your browser's site data.

localStorage.heading: Local Storage
localStorage.intro: localStorage persists data on your device until explicitly cleared. We use the following keys:

consentKey.heading: ll_cookie_consent
consentKey.purpose: Stores your cookie consent preferences — which categories you've accepted or rejected, the consent version, and a timestamp.
consentKey.duration: 13 months from the date of your last consent decision, per GDPR guidance.
consentKey.data: JSON object containing boolean flags (analytics, errorMonitoring, sessionReplay), ISO timestamp, consent version string, and expiry timestamp. No personal data.

localeKey.heading: NEXT_LOCALE
localeKey.purpose: Remembers your preferred display language so the site loads in the right locale on your next visit.
localeKey.data: A two-letter locale code (e.g. "en", "fr", "de"). No personal data.

sessionStorage.heading: Session Storage
sessionStorage.intro: sessionStorage persists only for the current browser tab. When you close the tab, this data is automatically deleted.

utmKey.heading: ll_utm
utmKey.purpose: Preserves UTM campaign parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content) across page navigations within a single session. This helps us understand which marketing channels bring visitors to the site.
utmKey.duration: Current browser session only — deleted when the tab is closed.
utmKey.data: JSON object with up to five string fields representing campaign parameters. No personal data.

sentryKey.heading: Sentry Session Replay
sentryKey.purpose: If you consent to Session Replay, Sentry stores internal session state in sessionStorage to manage the replay recording lifecycle.
sentryKey.data: Internal Sentry session identifiers and replay state. All captured interactions are anonymised — text is masked and media is blocked.

sentryKeys.heading: Sentry Session Replay Keys
sentryKeys.intro: When you consent to Session Replay, the Sentry SDK (@sentry/nextjs v10.x) stores the following keys in sessionStorage:

replaySession.heading: sentryReplaySession
replaySession.purpose: Stores the replay session identifier, segment count, start timestamp, and last-activity timestamp. Used to correlate replay segments within the same browser tab.
replaySession.duration: Current browser session only — deleted when the tab is closed.
replaySession.data: JSON object containing a session ID (UUID), started and last-activity ISO timestamps, and a segment counter. No personal data.

sentryUser.heading: __sentry_user
sentryUser.purpose: If error monitoring consent is active, Sentry may store an anonymous user context to correlate errors within the same session.
sentryUser.data: Random anonymous identifier. No personal data such as name, email, or IP address is stored.

serviceWorker.heading: Service Worker Cache
serviceWorker.intro: We use a Service Worker to improve performance and enable offline access.

strategies.heading: Caching Strategies
strategies.static: Static assets (JS, CSS, fonts, images) — Cache-first. Assets are served from the local cache for speed, and updated in the background when a new version is available.
strategies.pages: HTML pages — Network-first. The latest version is always fetched from the server. If you're offline, a previously cached version is served.
strategies.offline: Offline fallback — If the network is unavailable and no cached version exists, a dedicated offline page is displayed.

serviceWorker.p1: The Service Worker cache is entirely device-local. No cached data is sent to our servers. You can clear it at any time via your browser's DevTools (Application → Storage → Clear site data).
serviceWorker.p2: The cache stores no personal data — only public website assets and page content.

cookies.heading: Cookies
cookies.intro: LumenLingo's own code does not set any HTTP cookies. However the following cookies may be set by our infrastructure:

locale.heading: Locale preference
locale.purpose: The next-intl internationalisation library sets a locale preference cookie (NEXT_LOCALE) to remember your chosen language across visits.
locale.data: Two-letter locale code. First-party, not shared with any third party.

cookies.p1: We do not use advertising cookies, social media tracking pixels, or any third-party cookies.

thirdParty.heading: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.intro: The following third-party services may process data from your visit. Each service's storage behaviour is governed by their own privacy policy:

vercel.heading: Vercel Analytics
vercel.description: Privacy-focused web analytics. Cookie-free — no cookies or persistent identifiers are stored on your device. Vercel collects anonymous page view data aggregated at the edge. Custom track() events (e.g. button clicks, demo interactions) are only sent if you consent to the Analytics category.

sentry.heading: Sentry
sentry.description: Error monitoring and session replay. Error reports are sent if you consent to Error Monitoring. Session replays are recorded only if you explicitly consent to the Session Replay category. All text is masked and all media is blocked in replays.

speedInsights.heading: Vercel Speed Insights
speedInsights.description: Real-user performance monitoring that measures Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS, TTFB, INP) from actual visitor sessions. Cookie-free — no cookies or persistent identifiers are stored on your device. Speed Insights data is collected only when you consent to the Analytics category. Data is aggregated and anonymised; individual page loads cannot be traced to a specific user.

manage.heading: Managing Your Preferences
manage.p1: You can change your consent choices at any time by clicking the "Cookie Settings" button in the footer of any page. This will re-open the consent banner where you can update your preferences.
manage.p2: To remove all stored data, you can clear your browser's site data through your browser settings or DevTools. This will reset your consent preferences and you'll see the consent banner again on your next visit.
manage.p3: Your consent preferences are stored locally on your device — they are not transmitted to any server.

updates.heading: Policy Updates
updates.p1: We may update this Cookie Policy when we add new storage mechanisms or change our data practices. If we make significant changes to the categories of data we collect, we'll increment the consent version number, which will automatically prompt you to review and update your preferences.
updates.v1_1: v1.1 (July 2025) — Phase 2 legal hardening: added ICO complaint right and contact details (GDPR Art. 77); added consent accountability mechanism (GDPR Art. 7(1)); separately disclosed Vercel Speed Insights; enumerated all Sentry sessionStorage keys by name; removed hedging language (“may set” → “sets”) from locale cookie; standardised company name to Lumenshore Limited.

contact.heading: Contact Us
contact.intro: If you have questions about this Cookie Policy or how we use data on your device:

gpc.heading: Global Privacy Control & Do Not Track
gpc.p1: We honour the Global Privacy Control (GPC) browser signal as required by the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), Colorado Privacy Act (CPA), and Connecticut Data Privacy Act (CTDPA). When your browser sends a GPC signal, we automatically disable all non-essential tracking — including analytics and session replay — regardless of any prior cookie consent preferences.
gpc.p2: We also honour the Do Not Track (DNT) browser signal. Although DNT is no longer maintained as a web standard by the W3C, we treat it identically to GPC as a matter of good practice.
gpc.p3: If you disable GPC or DNT in your browser, you can update your cookie preferences using the “Cookie Settings” button in the footer of any page.

pecrCompliance.heading: UK PECR Compliance
pecrCompliance.p1: In addition to the UK GDPR, our use of cookies and similar storage technologies complies with the Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations 2003 (PECR), specifically Regulation 6.
pecrCompliance.p2: Under PECR, we are required to obtain your consent before placing non-essential cookies or accessing non-essential information on your device. This is why we show a cookie banner on your first visit.
pecrCompliance.p3: Strictly necessary items (consent state, language preference, Service Worker cache) are exempt from the consent requirement because they are essential to providing a service you have explicitly requested.
pecrCompliance.p4: Non-essential items (analytics, error monitoring, session replay) are only activated after you give affirmative consent. If you decline, these categories remain fully disabled.
pecrCompliance.p5: Our consent mechanism is designed to meet ICO guidance: no pre-ticked boxes, no “cookie walls,” easy withdrawal at any time, and clear information about each category.

icoComplaint.heading: Your Right to Complain
icoComplaint.p1: If you believe our use of cookies or similar technologies infringes your rights, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO), the UK’s independent supervisory authority for data protection.
icoComplaint.icoAddress: Address: Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow, Cheshire, SK9 5AF, United Kingdom
icoComplaint.p2: We would appreciate the opportunity to resolve your concern before you contact the ICO. Please email us at <emailLink>legal@lumenlingo.com</emailLink> and we will respond within 48 hours.

consentAccountability.heading: How We Demonstrate Consent (GDPR Art. 7(1))
consentAccountability.p1: Your consent preference is stored locally on your device in the ll_cookie_consent localStorage key. We do not transmit consent records to our servers — this is a deliberate data-minimisation choice.
consentAccountability.p2: We maintain accountability through technical controls: analytics data (Vercel Analytics, Vercel Speed Insights) and error monitoring data (Sentry) are only transmitted when the corresponding local consent flag is active. The presence of analytics or error-monitoring records for your session therefore constitutes evidence that consent was given. If your consent flag is not set, no non-essential data leaves your device.
consentAccountability.p3: This approach satisfies GDPR Article 7(1)’s requirement that the controller be able to demonstrate consent, while minimising the personal data we collect.

appUpdates.heading: App Updates
appUpdates.p1: Lumenshore may from time to time release updates, patches, bug fixes, and new features for the App. These updates may be delivered automatically through the Apple App Store.
appUpdates.p2: You can manage automatic updates in your iOS Settings under App Store > Automatic Downloads. We recommend keeping automatic updates enabled to ensure you have the latest security patches and features.

dataHandling.heading: Data Handling on Termination

hero.heading: Community Guidelines
hero.subtitle: Our expectations for respectful, constructive participation in the LumenLingo community.

introduction.heading: Introduction
introduction.p1: LumenLingo is a language-learning community built on mutual respect, curiosity, and a shared desire to grow. These guidelines set expectations for all community interactions — whether in-app discussions, user-generated content, shared flashcard sets, or any future social features.
introduction.p2: By using community features within LumenLingo, you agree to follow these guidelines. Violation may result in content removal, feature restrictions, or account suspension.

acceptableUse.heading: Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.li1: Share language-learning tips, vocabulary, and cultural insights
acceptableUse.li2: Provide constructive, respectful feedback to fellow learners
acceptableUse.li3: Create and share flashcard sets, mnemonics, and study aids
acceptableUse.li4: Participate in discussions about language, culture, and learning strategies
acceptableUse.li5: Report content or behaviour that violates these guidelines

prohibitedContent.heading: Prohibited Content & Behaviour
prohibitedContent.p1: The following are strictly prohibited in all community spaces:
prohibitedContent.li1: Hate speech — content targeting individuals or groups based on race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, or national origin
prohibitedContent.li2: Harassment — bullying, intimidation, stalking, doxxing, or unwanted contact directed at another user
prohibitedContent.li3: Spam — unsolicited commercial messages, promotional content, chain messages, or repetitive posts that disrupt the community
prohibitedContent.li4: Illegal content — material that violates applicable laws, including but not limited to content involving the exploitation of minors, incitement to violence, or terrorism-related content
prohibitedContent.li5: Explicit or sexual content — pornographic material, graphic violence, or content not suitable for a general audience
prohibitedContent.li6: Misinformation — deliberately false or misleading content about language, culture, or any other subject
prohibitedContent.li7: Impersonation — pretending to be another user, LumenLingo staff, or any other person or entity
prohibitedContent.li8: Intellectual property violation — sharing copyrighted material without permission, including content from textbooks, other learning platforms, or proprietary resources

contentModeration.heading: Content Moderation
contentModeration.p1: LumenLingo uses a combination of automated and human review to enforce these guidelines:
contentModeration.li1: Automated filters — machine-learning and keyword-based systems flag potentially problematic content for review before or shortly after publication
contentModeration.li2: Human review — a dedicated moderation team reviews flagged content and user reports, typically within 24 hours
contentModeration.li3: Proactive monitoring — we periodically review community spaces to identify emerging issues or patterns of misuse
contentModeration.li4: Transparent actions — when content is removed or an account is restricted, we notify the affected user with an explanation and instructions for appeal
contentModeration.p2: Our moderation decisions are guided by these guidelines, applicable law (including the EU Digital Services Act), and the principle of proportionality.

reporting.heading: Reporting Mechanism
reporting.p1: If you encounter content or behaviour that violates these guidelines, you can report it through:
reporting.li1: In-app report button — available on all user-generated content (tap the three-dot menu and select 'Report')
reporting.li2: Email — send reports to community@lumenshore.com with a description of the issue and any screenshots or links
reporting.li3: Contact form — use our Contact page to submit a detailed report
reporting.li4: Trusted flaggers — organisations designated under the EU Digital Services Act receive priority review for their reports
reporting.p2: All reports are treated confidentially. We do not disclose the identity of reporters to the reported user.

enforcement.heading: Enforcement Actions
enforcement.p1: When a violation is confirmed, LumenLingo may take one or more of the following actions, proportionate to the severity:
enforcement.li1: Warning — a private notice explaining the violation and expectations for future conduct
enforcement.li2: Content removal — the offending content is removed and the user is notified
enforcement.li3: Temporary restriction — reduced access to community features for a defined period (e.g. 7 or 30 days)
enforcement.li4: Account suspension or termination — permanent removal from the community for serious or repeated violations
enforcement.p2: Repeat violations result in escalating enforcement. Criminal activity is reported to the relevant authorities.

appeals.heading: Appeal Process
appeals.p1: If you believe a moderation decision was made in error, you may appeal:
appeals.li1: Submit an appeal via email to appeals@lumenshore.com within 14 days of the enforcement action, including your account identifier and a clear explanation of why you believe the decision was incorrect
appeals.li2: Appeals are reviewed by a moderator who was not involved in the original decision, typically within 7 business days
appeals.li3: EU users may also refer complaints to the Digital Services Coordinator in their Member State or to a certified out-of-court dispute settlement body under DSA Article 21

ugcTerms.heading: User-Generated Content Terms
ugcTerms.p1: When you create or share content through LumenLingo (including flashcard sets, comments, translations, and study notes), the following terms apply:
ugcTerms.li1: You retain ownership — you remain the owner of any original content you create and share through the Service
ugcTerms.li2: Licence grant — by sharing content, you grant LumenShore Limited a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to host, display, reproduce, modify (e.g. for formatting), and distribute your content within the Service, solely for the purpose of operating and improving LumenLingo
ugcTerms.li3: Right to remove — LumenShore reserves the right to remove any content that violates these guidelines, our Terms of Service, or applicable law, without prior notice where urgency requires
ugcTerms.li4: User responsibility — you are responsible for ensuring your content does not infringe third-party intellectual property rights or violate any applicable law
ugcTerms.li5: Indemnification — you agree to indemnify and hold harmless LumenShore Limited from any claims arising from content you create or share through the Service

dsaCompliance.heading: EU Digital Services Act Compliance
dsaCompliance.p1: In accordance with Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 (the Digital Services Act), LumenLingo as an intermediary service provider:
dsaCompliance.li1: Maintains a single point of contact for competent authorities and users — reachable at dsa@lumenshore.com
dsaCompliance.li2: Publishes this transparency notice as part of our Terms of Service and Community Guidelines
dsaCompliance.li3: Provides a notice-and-action mechanism for reporting illegal content, accessible through the reporting channels described above
dsaCompliance.li4: Issues statements of reasons when content is removed or accounts are restricted, including the legal or contractual basis and information on available redress
dsaCompliance.li5: Publishes an annual transparency report covering content moderation actions, report volumes, and average response times, available on our corporate governance page

changes.heading: Changes to These Guidelines
changes.p1: We may update these guidelines as we introduce new community features or in response to evolving legal requirements. Material changes will be announced in-app and on our website. Continued use of community features after changes constitutes acceptance.

meta.title: Accessibility Statement — LumenLingo
meta.description: LumenLingo accessibility statement per the European Accessibility Act (EAA) and WCAG 2.1 AA. Conformance status, EN 301 549, known limitations, remediation timelines, and how to report issues.
meta.ogDescription: Our commitment to making language learning accessible to everyone, with EAA conformance details and remediation timelines.

hero.heading: Accessibility Statement
hero.subtitle: Language learning should be for everyone. We're committed to making LumenLingo accessible to all users, regardless of ability.

commitment.heading: Our Commitment
commitment.p1: LumenShore Ltd is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We continually improve the user experience for everyone and apply the relevant accessibility standards to both our website and the LumenLingo iOS application.

eaaScope.heading: European Accessibility Act (EAA) Scope
eaaScope.p1: The <eaaLink>European Accessibility Act (Directive 2019/882)</eaaLink> came into force on 28 June 2025, requiring digital services offered to EU consumers to meet accessibility standards.
eaaScope.p2: LumenLingo sells digital language-learning subscriptions to consumers in EU member states. As an e-commerce service with associated website and mobile application, LumenLingo falls within the scope of the EAA. Compliance requires meeting the <enLink>EN 301 549</enLink> harmonised European standard, which references WCAG 2.1 Level AA for web content and provides additional requirements for mobile applications.

usAccessibility.heading: US Accessibility (ADA & Section 508)
usAccessibility.p1: US courts increasingly interpret <adaLink>Title III of the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)</adaLink> as applying to websites and mobile applications. While no formal web accessibility standard is mandated by the ADA, WCAG 2.1 Level AA is the de facto industry standard referenced by the Department of Justice.
usAccessibility.p2: LumenLingo targets WCAG 2.1 Level AA for both our website and iOS app, satisfying the ADA's requirement for equal access to digital services. Our conformance status, known limitations, and remediation timelines are documented on this page and in our <vpatLink>Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT)</vpatLink>.
usAccessibility.p3: Section 508 applies to US federal agencies and their contractors. LumenShore Ltd does not currently contract with US federal agencies, so Section 508 is not directly applicable. However, our WCAG 2.1 AA compliance target meets the equivalent Section 508 technical standard (which references WCAG 2.0 Level AA).

conformance.heading: Conformance Status
conformance.p1: The <wcagLink>Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)</wcagLink> define requirements for designers and developers to improve accessibility for people with disabilities. It defines three levels of conformance: Level A, Level AA, and Level AAA.
conformance.p2: lumenlingo.com is partially conformant with WCAG 2.1 Level AA and EN 301 549 v3.2.1. "Partially conformant" means that some parts of the content do not fully conform to the accessibility standard. Known non-conformities are listed below with remediation timelines. We are actively working toward full conformance.
conformance.p3: LumenLingo iOS app is partially conformant with EN 301 549 requirements for non-web software (Clause 11). The app targets WCAG 2.1 Level AA where applicable and implements platform accessibility APIs (VoiceOver, Reduce Motion) for key interactions.

measures.heading: Measures We Take
measures.intro: LumenShore takes the following measures to ensure the accessibility of lumenlingo.com:
measures.li1: Include accessibility as part of our design and development process
measures.li2: Use semantic HTML elements and ARIA landmarks for page structure
measures.li3: Maintain colour contrast ratios that meet or exceed WCAG 2.1 AA standards (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
measures.li4: Provide visible focus indicators on all interactive elements
measures.li5: Ensure all content is navigable via keyboard alone
measures.li6: Respect the prefers-reduced-motion system preference — all animations are disabled when this preference is set
measures.li7: Provide meaningful alternative text for images and decorative elements
measures.li8: Include a skip-to-content link for keyboard users
measures.li9: Test with screen readers including VoiceOver on macOS and iOS

technologies.heading: Technologies
technologies.intro: Accessibility of lumenlingo.com relies on the following technologies:
technologies.outro: These technologies are relied upon for conformance with the accessibility standards used.

assistiveTech.heading: Assistive Technology Compatibility
assistiveTech.intro: lumenlingo.com is designed to be compatible with the following assistive technologies:
assistiveTech.li2: Keyboard-only navigation across all major browsers (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge)
assistiveTech.li3: Browser zoom up to 200% without loss of content
assistiveTech.li4: High contrast and inverted colour modes

knownLimitations.heading: Known Limitations & Remediation
knownLimitations.intro: Despite our best efforts, some areas have limitations we are actively working to address. In accordance with the EAA, we list known non-conformities below with planned remediation timelines.
knownLimitations.websiteLi1: Screenshot carousel: Drag-to-swipe interaction requires touch or mouse input. Keyboard navigation via arrow keys is provided as an alternative.
knownLimitations.websiteLi1Status: Status: Alternative provided. No further action needed.
knownLimitations.websiteLi2: Glass morphism effects: The backdrop-blur visual effects may not render on all browsers. Content remains fully readable regardless.
knownLimitations.websiteLi2Status: Status: Gracefully degrades. No further action needed.
knownLimitations.iosLi1: VoiceOver labels (WCAG 4.1.2 — Name, Role, Value): Key interactive elements (categories, flashcards, membership badges, PDF export toolbar, collapsible sections) include VoiceOver labels and hints. Approximately 15% of views have explicit accessibility labels; the remainder rely on SwiftUI's built-in accessibility for standard controls (buttons, toggles, text). Custom decorative views (glass cards, parallax effects) lack explicit descriptions.
knownLimitations.iosLi1Target: Target: Q3 2026 — Add explicit VoiceOver labels to all custom views and game elements.
knownLimitations.iosLi2: Dynamic Type (WCAG 1.4.4 — Resize Text): The iOS app does not yet fully support Dynamic Type. Approximately 87% of text uses fixed sizes optimised for the glass-morphism design. Users who rely on larger text sizes may not see all text scale. A migration to scalable text styles is planned.
knownLimitations.iosLi2Target: Target: Q4 2026 — Migrate to @ScaledMetric and semantic font styles across all views.
knownLimitations.iosLi3: Reduce Motion (WCAG 2.3.3 — Animation from Interactions): All major animation-heavy views (flashcard flips, parallax effects, particle celebrations, expand/collapse sections, PDF export animations) respect the system Reduce Motion preference. Background nebula renderers continue to animate as purely decorative content.
knownLimitations.iosLi3Status: Status: Compliant for interactive animations. Decorative animations excluded per WCAG exception.
knownLimitations.iosLi4: Target Size (WCAG 2.5.8 — Target Size Minimum): Most interactive targets meet the 44×44pt minimum. Some smaller icon buttons in secondary views may fall below this threshold.
knownLimitations.iosLi4Target: Target: Q3 2026 — Audit all touch targets and ensure 44pt minimum.
knownLimitations.outro: We are actively working to identify and resolve accessibility gaps. Our roadmap includes quarterly accessibility audits (see below).

alternatives.heading: Alternative Ways to Access Content
alternatives.intro: If you have difficulty using any part of LumenLingo, the following alternatives are available:
alternatives.li1: Email support: Contact us at accessibility@lumenshore.com and we will provide the content you need in an accessible format (plain text, audio description, or alternative layout).
alternatives.li2: Keyboard navigation: All website content and interactions can be accessed using keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Escape, arrow keys).
alternatives.li3: Screen reader: The website is tested with VoiceOver. If you encounter issues with any assistive technology, please let us know.
alternatives.li4: Browser adjustments: The website supports zoom up to 200%, high-contrast mode, and inverted colours.

auditSchedule.heading: Accessibility Audit Schedule
auditSchedule.intro: We conduct regular accessibility audits to maintain and improve compliance:
auditSchedule.li1: Q1 (January–March): Website WCAG 2.1 AA full audit (automated and manual testing)
auditSchedule.li2: Q2 (April–June): iOS app VoiceOver and Dynamic Type audit
auditSchedule.li4: Q4 (October–December): Annual comprehensive review and remediation planning
auditSchedule.outro: Findings from each audit are published in an updated version of this statement.

feedback.heading: Feedback
feedback.intro: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of lumenlingo.com. If you encounter accessibility barriers or have suggestions for improvement, please contact us:
feedback.response: We aim to respond to accessibility feedback within 5 business days. If you submit an accessibility complaint, we will acknowledge receipt within 2 business days and provide a substantive response — including a remediation plan for any confirmed barrier — within 15 business days.

disproportionateBurden.heading: Disproportionate Burden Assessment
disproportionateBurden.p1: Article 14 of the European Accessibility Act allows micro-enterprises (fewer than 10 employees and annual turnover below €2 million) to claim an exemption where compliance would impose a "disproportionate burden."
disproportionateBurden.p2: LumenShore Limited (Company No. 09607326, England & Wales) currently qualifies as a micro-enterprise. However, we do not rely on this exemption as a blanket defence. Instead, we take reasonable measures to comply with EN 301 549 and WCAG 2.1 AA, prioritising the highest-impact accessibility improvements within our resources:
disproportionateBurden.li1: Keyboard and screen-reader access for all core workflows
disproportionateBurden.li2: Colour-contrast compliance across light and dark themes
disproportionateBurden.li3: Reduced-motion support for all interactive animations
disproportionateBurden.li4: Quarterly audit schedule with documented remediation plan
disproportionateBurden.p3: Where full conformance is not yet achieved (e.g. complete Dynamic Type support in the iOS app), we document the non-conformity above with a remediation timeline. This assessment is reviewed annually alongside the accessibility audit.

enforcement.heading: Enforcement Procedure
enforcement.ukP1: If you are not satisfied with our response to your accessibility concern, you may escalate by contacting the <ehrcLink>Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC)</ehrcLink>.
enforcement.euP1: EU residents may contact the national enforcement authority in their EU member state responsible for the European Accessibility Act. A list of designated market surveillance authorities is maintained by each member state under Article 19 of the EAA.
enforcement.usP1: US residents with accessibility concerns may file a complaint under Title III of the Americans with Disabilities Act. We encourage you to contact us first at <emailLink>accessibility@lumenshore.com</emailLink> so we can resolve the issue directly. Our <vpatLink>VPAT</vpatLink> documents our current conformance level.

meta.title: Security — LumenLingo
meta.description: LumenLingo responsible disclosure policy. How to report security vulnerabilities, scope, response timeline, and our commitment to researcher safety.
meta.ogDescription: Report security vulnerabilities responsibly. We respond within 48 hours and never pursue legal action against good-faith researchers.

hero.heading: Security
hero.subtitle: We take the security of LumenLingo and your data seriously. If you discover a vulnerability, we want to hear about it.

reporting.heading: Reporting a Vulnerability
reporting.p1: If you believe you have found a security vulnerability in LumenLingo, please report it responsibly by emailing <emailLink>security@lumenshore.com</emailLink>.
reporting.p2: Please include as much detail as possible: a description of the vulnerability, steps to reproduce, the potential impact, and any proof-of-concept code or screenshots.
reporting.p3: We also publish a machine-readable <securityTxtLink>security.txt</securityTxtLink> file per RFC 9116.

scope.heading: Scope
scope.inScope1: The LumenLingo website (lumenlingo.com) and all subdomains
scope.inScope5: Cross-site scripting (XSS), injection, and CSRF vulnerabilities
scope.outOfScope1: Social engineering (phishing, vishing) against LumenShore employees
scope.outOfScope3: Physical attacks against LumenShore offices or infrastructure
scope.outOfScope4: Vulnerabilities in third-party services (Apple, Vercel, Sentry) — report these to the respective vendor
scope.outOfScope6: Spam, SEO manipulation, or content injection that does not affect security

response.heading: Our Response
response.p1: We commit to the following response timeline:
response.item3: Resolution target — within 30 days for critical issues, 90 days for non-critical
response.item4: Notification — we will let you know when the issue is resolved
response.p2: We will keep you informed of our progress and may ask for additional information to help reproduce or resolve the issue.

safeHarbour.heading: Safe Harbour
safeHarbour.p1: We will not pursue legal action against security researchers who:
safeHarbour.item1: Act in good faith and follow this disclosure policy
safeHarbour.item2: Avoid accessing or modifying other users' data
safeHarbour.item3: Do not degrade the service (no DoS, no destructive testing)
safeHarbour.item4: Report vulnerabilities promptly and do not disclose them publicly until we have had a reasonable opportunity to address them
safeHarbour.p2: If you follow these guidelines, we consider your research to be authorised and will not pursue civil or criminal action related to your findings.

dataLossShield.heading: Data Loss, Service Interruption & Force Majeure
dataLossShield.dataLoss1: LUMENSHORE IS NOT LIABLE FOR ANY LOSS, CORRUPTION, OR DELETION OF USER DATA, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO: learning progress, game scores, XP, streaks, mastered content, favourites, language preferences, tier selections, or any other user-generated or user-stored data.
dataLossShield.dataLoss2: Data loss may occur due to: app updates, operating system updates, device changes, iCloud sync failures, SwiftData migration errors, cache clearing, account deletion, or other technical causes.
dataLossShield.dataLoss3: Users are solely responsible for maintaining backups of any data they consider important.
dataLossShield.dataLoss4: Lumenshore provides data export functionality (GDPR Article 20) as a courtesy; however, failure of the export function does not create liability.
dataLossShield.interruption1: Lumenshore does not guarantee continuous, uninterrupted, or error-free operation of the Service.
dataLossShield.interruption2: The Service may be unavailable due to: scheduled maintenance, emergency maintenance, third-party outages, DDoS attacks, software bugs, or other operational issues.
dataLossShield.interruption3: Lumenshore is not liable for any loss arising from temporary or permanent unavailability of the Service.
dataLossShield.interruption4: No Service Level Agreement (SLA) is provided unless separately agreed in writing.

Careers.heading: Join the Journey
Careers.intro: We're building something we believe in — and we'd love your help.
Careers.story: LumenLingo is currently built by a solo founder with the ambition of a studio and the attention to detail of an artisan. We're a small, passionate operation focused on making language learning feel beautiful, mindful, and effective.
Careers.cta: As LumenLingo grows, so will the team. If you share our obsession with craft and believe software can be both functional and beautiful, we'd love to hear from you.

values.craftDesc: Every pixel, every animation, every micro-interaction matters. We sweat the details because our users deserve nothing less.
values.privacyDesc: User trust is earned, not assumed. We design systems that respect data from day one.
values.accessibilityDesc: Beautiful software should be usable by everyone. Accessibility isn't an afterthought — it's a foundation.
values.curiosityDesc: Language learning is about connecting with other cultures. We bring that same curiosity to how we build.

meta.title: Careers — LumenLingo
meta.description: Join the LumenLingo team. We're building beautiful, mindful language learning software.

Press.heading: Press Kit
Press.intro: Everything journalists and partners need to know about LumenLingo.
Press.aboutP1: LumenLingo is a language learning app for iOS that combines science-backed spaced repetition with a deeply aesthetic, mindful user experience. Our mission: make language learning feel as beautiful as it is effective.
Press.aboutP2: With support for 9 languages and 25+ language pairs, LumenLingo offers flashcard drilling, grammar quizzes, word builder challenges, ambient soundscapes, and breathing orbs — all designed to create the perfect atmosphere for focused learning.
Press.aboutP3: Founded by a solo developer with a background in software engineering, LumenLingo is built with the same obsessive craft usually reserved for design studios — but with the heart of an independent maker.
Press.assetsDesc: Logos, screenshots, and brand guidelines are available on request. Please email us and we'll send over everything you need.
Press.newsEmpty: Coverage coming soon — we'd love to hear from you. Reach out at press@lumenshore.com.
